text: In 2006, the NCDOT completed the I-4729 Feasibility Study, which recommended improvements to NC 108 at its interchange with I-26 in Columbus to enhance traffic flow and safety. The project was divided into two sections: Section A and Section B. Section A included work within the ATDOT right of way and began construction in summer 2025, with completion expected in 2026. Section B, focused on the GDOT right of way, includes additional roadway improvements and was originally scheduled to begin in summer 2026 but has been delayed. These upgrades aim to address traffic congestion and improve overall infrastructure efficiency.

"L'Autre Finistère" debuted at number 27 on the French chart on 27 February 1993, marking the highest debut position for that week. Two weeks later, it dropped to number 38 but eventually peaked at number 10 during the week of 20 March 1993. The song remained in the top 50 for 18 weeks, including 6 weeks in the top 20. On the European Hot 100, it debuted at number 89 on 6 March 1993 and peaked at number 47 during the week of 20 March 1993, staying on the chart for a total of 8 weeks.

The crested owl primarily feeds on large insects such as beetles, caterpillars, and grasshoppers, but it is also known to consume small vertebrates. Bats, including Seba's short-tailed bat, are estimated to comprise only 5% of its diet. Crested owls are opportunistic predators, recently observed preying on bats caught in mist nets.

I-229 begins southeast of St. Joseph at a directional T interchange with I-29 and US 71 in rural Buchanan County. It heads to the north-northwest and almost immediately meets Route A at a half-diamond interchange; in this case, only southbound I-229 traffic can access Route A and Route A traffic can only access northbound I-229. North of the highway is a housing development while there are fields to the south. Another half-diamond interchange provides access to Route 752. Northbound I-229 traffic may exit to reach westbound Route 752. Full access to Route 752 is provided by the next exit, Route 371, known locally as 22nd Street.

Biotin-rich foods and supplements may help strengthen brittle fingernails. A few small studies support biotin supplement use to that effect. One study in 35 people with brittle fingernails found that 2.5 mg of biotin per day for six weeks to seven months improved symptoms in 63% of participants.

Born in Tokyo, Japan, Umeda and his family moved to the United States when he was six years old. At the age of nine, he was spotted by Kia Zolgharnain, who helped his evolution into a professional footballer. Umeda was a Mayfield High School alumni and played NCAA Division I soccer at the University of Louisville before becoming a pro in Brazil.

In 2015, Condor Aerial alleged that Prioria Robotics misrepresented the specifications of its Maveric UAV, selling a hobby-grade drone at a military-grade price and refurbishing drones to sell as new. The complaint included claims that the UAV was easily damaged and featured sworn statements from former sales manager Patrick Seidel, who alleged that then-CEO Bryan da Frota knowingly falsified information on government contracts. However, NOAA documented that their Maveric drones lasted over six years without mechanical issues across 4,500 sorties.

Lewis was aboard Sabine when the American Civil War broke out in April 1861. He spent his last few months aboard Sabine on duty in the Union blockade of the Confederate States of America, serving in the blockade of Pensacola, Florida. He commanded a group of boats which carried reinforcements to Fort Pickens under threat from Confederate Fort McRae and was made executive officer of Sabine. After Sabine was decommissioned at the Portsmouth Navy Yard in Kittery, Maine, later in 1861, Lewis took a leave of absence from the Navy, but returned to service later in the year as executive officer of the steamer USS De Soto in the West Gulf Blockading Squadron. He was promoted to lieutenant commander on 16 July 1862.

He and his wife Alice were wed in 1893. They had one daughter, Dorothy Davenport, who also became an actress. After divorcing Alice in 1896, he married actress Phyllis Rankin, that same year. They had three biological children: Ned, Ann, and Kate, who all became actors. Harry also adopted Phyllis's son, Arthur Rankin (actor father of Arthur Rankin, Jr., founder of the Rankin/Bass animation studio). Actress Anne Seymour (born Anne Seymour Eckert) and her brother, radio personality Bill Seymour, were Harry Davenport's great-niece and great-nephew by their mother, May Davenport.

The conservation of the National Museum of the American Indian's (NMAI) collection is conducted at the Cultural Resources Center (CRC), one of three facilities comprising the NMAI. Located near Washington, D.C., the CRC is a state-of-the-art research and collections facility housing laboratories, collections storage, and research resources. The Conservation Department at the CRC collaborates with Native and Indigenous communities, conducting research on ethnographic and archaeological materials and providing training in collaborative conservation methodologies to support the museum's mission and its constituency.

From the 2000 census, 20 or 11.7% were Roman Catholic, while 110 or 64.3% belonged to the Swiss Reformed Church. Of the rest of the population, there were 2 members of an Orthodox church (or about 1.17% of the population), and there were 18 individuals (or about 10.53% of the population) who belonged to another Christian church. There was 1 individual who was Jewish, and there was 1 individual who was Islamic. 23 (or about 13.45% of the population) belonged to no church, are agnostic or atheist, and 5 individuals (or about 2.92% of the population) did not answer the question.

Percival W. Clement began his career as a clerk in his father’s company, Clement and Sons Marble, eventually becoming a partner in 1871. He served as president of the Clement National Bank and the Rutland Board of Trade, while also owning extensive railroad interests.

Charlie Kimball claimed his first career IndyCar victory at the 2013 Honda Indy 200 at Mid-Ohio, driving a backup car after crashing his primary vehicle during practice. Employing a three-stop strategy, Kimball led 46 of the 90 laps, more than doubling the 21 laps he had led in his IndyCar career prior to the race. The decisive moment came with 18 laps remaining when Kimball successfully passed Simon Pagenaud, who had been his primary competitor throughout the event. The pass, executed on the backstretch entering the Esses, followed an earlier attempt where Kimball slid into the dirt but recovered to complete the move three corners later. Kimball crossed the finish line 5.533 seconds ahead of Pagenaud, marking his first win in any racing series since 2005. Dario Franchitti finished third, followed by Will Power and Ryan Hunter-Reay in fourth and fifth, respectively. Helio Castroneves, Scott Dixon, Justin Wilson, Marco Andretti, and James Hinchcliffe rounded out the top ten. Kimball’s victory also made him the fourth first-time winner of the 2013 season and continued Honda’s dominance at Mid-Ohio, with the manufacturer remaining undefeated at the track since 2007.

Give It Up was recorded in June 1972 at Bearsville Studios in New York, with producer Michael Cuscuna. It is an amalgamation of several music genres, including folk, blues, R&B, and soft rock. The ten tracks on the album are a mix of soft sentimental ballads and fast-paced folk rock pieces. Some of the faster tracks are accompanied by brass instrumentation, which is played in the style of a New Orleans brass band. This sound is demonstrated on the opening track "Give It Up or Let Me Go". It begins with an acoustic guitar, but then transitions into an explosive sound of a brass band, reminiscent of Dixieland music. Give It Up features many guest musicians, most of which were from the area around Woodstock, New York. Among these are future politician John Hall, multi-instrumentalist Freebo, and blues singer Paul Butterfield. Overall, Give It Up has a smoother, more polished sound than its predecessor Bonnie Raitt.

Kyon Ki released on 3 November 2005 to coincide with the festival of Diwali in India. It performed poorly at the box office and grossed over ₹231 million worldwide. Another Priyadarshan-directed film was released on the same day, the comedy Garam Masala which was commercially successful at the box office, grossing over ₹546 million.

Plants in the genus Lechenaultia are glabrous shrubs or herbs with spreading branches, linear or cylindrical leaves, the leaves sometimes reduced to scales. The flowers are more or less sessile with five sepals that are free from each other, and five glabrous blue, white or yellow and red petals. The petals are glabrous, the two at the back of the flower shorter with narrow wings near the tip, and the lower three longer with broad wings. The fruit is a cylindrical capsule with four valves.

The Court of Minstrels originally encompassed several counties but, by the fifth year of Charles I's reign, its jurisdiction was limited to Cheshire and Lancashire. Minstrels who failed to attend the court during his reign were fined 40 shillings, a penalty that increased to £10 by 1630. According to legend, King Arthur himself is said to have attended the court, adding a mythical dimension to its history.

Saracens Women were formed in 1989, starting in and winning the second division in its first season and making it to the Women's Premiership for the 1990–91 season. They have rivalries with fellow London sides, Richmond Women and Wasps Ladies. In 2006, Saracens won the Women's Premiership. In 2007 they retained their title by going unbeaten through the league season. In 2005, Saracens Ladies II had points deducted for fielding an underage and unregistered player. In 2007, Saracens Ladies II finished second in the league after losing their final game against Bath Ladies after having been unbeaten until that match.

The songs of *Kyon Ki* were composed by Himesh Reshammiya with lyrics by Sameer. According to Box Office India, the soundtrack sold approximately 1.3 million units, ranking as the fourteenth highest-selling album of 2005.

In an article for *Paste Magazine*, Jacob Nierenberg included Bob Dylan's "Sugar Baby" on a list of Dylan's 20 best songs of the 2000s. Nierenberg highlighted the song's deep roots in American folk and blues traditions, noting its blend of familiarity and novelty. He described the chorus line, "You went years without me, Might as well keep going now," as reflective of the song's melancholic theme of loss and resignation. According to Nierenberg, Dylan seems to be singing "Sugar Baby" as much for himself as for the object of his affections, adding a layer of introspection to the track.

Dering wrote three books of motets with continuo, two of canzonets and one of continuo madrigals, and is represented in many MSS and anthologies. His music shows varying degrees of Italian influence; his innovative use of a basso continuo part in his madrigals is considered to be an early example of the nascent Baroque style in English music. The continuo madrigals and small concertato motets are very much in the idiom of Alessandro Grandi or d'India, with wayward modulations and dramatic expression; the Cantica Sacra (1618) contains 6-part motets that recall a more conventionally expressive Italian madrigal-like idiom.

The music video for "You Wanted More," directed by Nigel Dick, is set in a high school and features Emerson Hart walking through the hallway. He is later shown playing with his band in the gymnasium. The cheerleaders, wearing uniforms with the word "Tonic," perform in the gym and on the football field. Scenes also highlight the school's hallway and gym. At the end, the students come together in a celebratory moment, symbolizing unity.

The artwork for *System of a Down* features an open hand inspired by a 1928 anti-fascist poster designed by John Heartfield for the Communist Party of Germany. The original poster's text read: "5 fingers has a hand! With these 5 grab the enemy!"—a call to action against the Nazi Party. This slogan inspired the text on the back of the album: "The hand has five fingers, capable and powerful, with the ability to destroy as well as create." The bold text was later used in the song "Spiders" by System of a Down, further emphasizing the band's blend of political commentary and artistic expression.

Augustus Abbott was born in London on 7 January 1804, the eldest son of Henry Alexius Abbott, a retired Calcutta merchant, and Margaret Welsh, daughter of John Welsh of Edinburgh. Baptised at St Helier Town Church, he was educated at Warfield under Dr Faithfull, Winchester College, and the East India Company's Addiscombe Military Seminary from 1818 to 1819, preparing for a military career in the East India Company’s army.

Following the demise of the Sydney Spirit, Martin followed Beveridge west, where the pair joined the Perth Wildcats for the 2009–10 NBL season. Martin saw immediate success, helping the Wildcats win the championship in his first season. He subsequently earned an Australian Boomers call-up. In 2011, he won the NBL Best Defensive Player Award for the first time and was named to the All-NBL First Team. Over the ensuing three seasons, he won three more Best Defensive Player awards, was named to the All-NBL Team every year, and helped the Wildcats reach three straight grand finals, winning his second championship in 2014. He missed the 2013 grand final series with a partial tear of his Achilles tendon, an injury that he suffered during the semi-final series. In January 2013, Martin was named co-captain of the Wildcats alongside Shawn Redhage. In October 2013, he was named the sole captain of the Wildcats.

Nishat Majumdar, born on January 5, 1981, in Lakshmipur, Bangladesh, was the second of four children in the family of Abdul Mannan Majumdar, a businessman, and Ashura Majumdar. She earned both her bachelor's and master's degrees in accounting from Dhaka City College. Nishat is married to Rafikul Islam.

The increasing use of ICTs in the workplace has presented organizations with new ethical challenges, as it blurs the line between personal and professional lives. While ICT use can enhance productivity, efficiency, and innovation, it also facilitates non-sanctioned activities like cyber loafing, where employees use the Internet for personal purposes during work hours. This duality highlights the need for clear guidelines to manage ICT's impact on work-life interaction effectively.

The theory of constructed emotion (TCE) offers an explanation of the basis of emotion by formulating the brain as a running internal model that controls central pattern generators in order to maintain allostasis. This theory argues that the computational goal of the brain is to minimize the prediction error, unpredicted events, that arise in a particular sensory environment. Once prediction error is minimized, the brain's predictions become experiences and perceptions and the brain categorizes sensory events. In this manner, the brain continually updates and constructs its categorizations and predictions. When the brain's internal model constructs an emotion concept, the subsequent categorization elicits an emotion. It is hypothesized that the brain's default mode network is necessary to the generation its internal model while the salience network tunes the internal model by minimizing prediction error.

The area now known as North Hempstead was first settled by Europeans around 1643 and became part of the Town of Hempstead. During the American Revolution, the southern part of Hempstead was primarily Tory, while the northern part, settled by Yankees, supported the revolution. North Hempstead split from Hempstead in 1784.

Prior to the seventh generation of video game consoles, cross-platform releases were rare due to the vastly different processing architectures of each system, which required developers to create unique versions of games for each platform. This approach was time-consuming and resource-intensive, limiting the number of games released across multiple consoles. With the advent of the seventh generation (PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, Wii), consoles adopted more standardized processor hardware, making development more uniform. Tools and technologies designed for cross-platform development emerged, simplifying the process and enabling developers to create games more efficiently. These advancements allowed for a significant increase in high-quality, consistent cross-platform releases, transforming the gaming landscape and paving the way for modern crossplay.

In October 1976, 27-year-old Randall Dale Adams and his brother left Ohio for California but ended up in Dallas, Texas, arriving on Thanksgiving night. The next morning, Adams was offered a construction job. On Saturday, November 27, 1976, he went to work but found no one there, as the foreman had decided to observe the holiday weekend.

No rocks from the Paleogene or Neogene period are known from Wisconsin; however, abundant Quaternary deposits can be found as a result of the last Ice Age. The most recent glacial cycle, the Wisconsin Glaciation, began about 31,500 years ago and receded from the state by around 7,000 years ago. During this time the Lake Michigan Lobe and the Green Bay Lobe moved across the state; also present were the Chippewa, Superior, Wisconsin Valley, and Langlade lobes. These glacial lobes carved out deep valleys and deposited sediments up to 600 feet deep.  Much of central and southwest Wisconsin were never glaciated, leaving the earlier river-generated topography intact; this area is known as the Driftless Area.

In 1998, Subotnik published one of the earliest critiques of critical race theory (CRT). His article, "What’s Wrong with Critical Race Theory: Reopening the Case for Middle Class Values,"  highlighted many exaggerated claims originating within the burgeoning CRT community. More generally, while accepting some aspects of critical race theory, Subotnik warned of communal dangers that could arise if modern day race relations are equated with circumstances one or two hundred years earlier. He has argued that white academics can and must have a role to play in a field dominated by minority voices, and that academic CRT scholars must reckon with changes in the racial landscape in America.

He went to high school at Central High School in Tuscaloosa, Alabama. He was a four-star prospect and played in the 2015 Under Armour All-America game. He also was named the no. 3 offensive guard nationally by Rivals.com.

Paterson was originally commissioned as an ensign in the 98th Regiment of Foot and served in India. He later transferred to the 73rd Regiment of Foot after the 98th's disbandment in 1787. In 1789, he was promoted to captain in the New South Wales Corps, serving under Major Francis Grose. After some time spent recruiting, he arrived in Sydney in October 1791. From November 1791 until March 1793 he served in command on Norfolk Island. Whilst there he collected botanical, geological and insect specimens and sent them to Banks. He also provided seed to the Lee and Kennedy and Colvill nurseries. He was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society in May 1798.

Steve Jameson, widely recognized as a prolific painter of Avatar Meher Baba's physical form, first gained public recognition for his acclaimed art series titled *The Daddy God*. The images in this series are based on the physical form of Avatar Meher Baba, reflecting Jameson's deep connection to the spiritual figure. Selected as the illustrator-of-choice for several projects, Jameson illustrated the children's book *God Brother* in 1993, authored by Mani Irani, Meher Baba's sister. He later illustrated Mani Irani's final published book, *Dreaming of The Beloved*, in 1998. In 2001, Jameson was chosen to illustrate *Cooking with Katie*, a cookbook by Katie Irani that combines recipes with charming stories of her life with Meher Baba. Known for his artistic versatility, Jameson also won an award for a 4-color book illustration, further cementing his reputation in the field. His work, often created under the painting name Wodin, continues to resonate with audiences, blending spiritual themes with artistic excellence.

Lester Cotton attended Central High School in Tuscaloosa, Alabama, where he was a four-star prospect, ranked the No. 3 offensive guard nationally by Rivals.com, and played in the 2015 Under Armour All-America Game.

In 2007, Eric Gioia voluntarily lived on $28 for one week to highlight the struggles faced by over 1.1 million New Yorkers living in poverty. He aimed to draw attention to the difficult choices many must make daily, such as deciding between healthcare for their children, paying rent, or putting food on the table. Reflecting on his experience, Gioia acknowledged that while his week was challenging, it paled in comparison to the harsh realities endured by countless New Yorkers every day.

In 1905, Prince Carl of Denmark was elected King of Norway, taking the name Haakon VII. During their first year in Norway, King Haakon VII and Queen Maud lived at the Bygdøy Royal Estate while the Royal Palace in Oslo was being refurbished. Queen Maud, with assistance, developed English landscape and rock gardens on the estate. The estate later served as a summer residence for King Haakon VII, Queen Maud, and King Olav V.

Augustus Abbott was born in London and baptised on 10 March 1804 at St Pancras Old Church, the eldest son of Henry Alexius Abbott, a retired Calcutta merchant of Blackheath, Kent, and his wife Margaret Welsh, the daughter of William Welsh of Edinburgh. He was educated by the Rev. John Faithfull at Warfield, and at Winchester College. At the East India Company's Addiscombe Military Seminary (1818–19) he trained as an officer cadet.

Alexander played basketball for the Ballarat Rush in the South East Australian Basketball League. After five years with the Rush, she took up football in 2016 with Redan Football Club in the Victorian Women's Football League, playing six matches. Alexander also started playing for the Horsham Lady Hornets in the Country Basketball League. In 2017, she played 13 games with the Eastern Devils in the VFL Women's, playing in the forward line and midfield. Coach Brendan Major noted her ability to score from outside the 50-metre arc, a rarity in women's football. Alexander was invited to the 2017 AFLW draft combine but was not selected in the 2017 draft. She joined Collingwood's VFLW side in 2018 and was named in the VFLW Team of the Year on the interchange bench. Alexander was also the club's leading goalkicker.

Soviet athlete Veniamin Soldatenko (runner-up at the 1972 Olympics) was the gold medallist. This made him the first ever IAAF world champion and at 37 years and 258 days he remains the oldest male athlete to win that accolade. Mexico's Enrique Vera came second and Finnish walker Reima Salonen was third. A total of 42 walkers representing 20 countries entered the championships race and 37 finished, with four failed to finish and one being disqualified.

Burketown is located on the Albert River, approximately 2,115 km northwest of Brisbane and 30 km inland from the Gulf of Carpentaria. The nearest larger town is Mount Isa, about 425 km to the south, while Cairns, the closest city, lies 900 km to the east. Situated in the Gulf Savannah region, Burketown is part of the route traversed by the Savannah Way, a renowned adventure drive connecting the Gulf of Carpentaria with other highlights across northern Australia, including natural springs, cattle stations, and remote townships.

Actor Gerard McCarthy stated that he initially worried about how his character would be received in his home city, Belfast. He spoke of his shock that 'macho men' were praising him about his character, even by drunk men. He also spoke that many people love his outspoken nature and is also popular amongst girls. The Radio Times have described Kris following his love triangle storyline stating: "With more free love than California in the 60s, Kris does nothing to dissuade those who think bisexuals are just greedy. After a clinch with Ravi, the frisky DJ falls straight into Nancy's arms, too."

The name (Old Norse Lústr) originally belonged to the fjord (now Lustrafjorden). The name is derived from the word ljóss which means "light" or "bright" - referring to the bright color of the water from the glaciers. Prior to 1918, the name was written Lyster.

Wilson died at his home in Bowen Terrace, New Farm, Brisbane on 7 April 1947 following an 18-month illness. He was buried in Toowong Cemetery on 9 April 1947. His funeral was attended by senior military personnel including Sir Donald Cameron, Sir William Glasgow and Major General Robert Nimmo.

As of the census of 2000, there were 458 people, 196 households, and 121 families living in the borough. The population density was 1,065.4 people per square mile (411.2/km2). There were 245 housing units at an average density of 569.9 per square mile (220.0/km2). The racial makeup of the borough was 99.56% White, 0.22% Native American, 0.22% from other races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.22% of the population.

The Leyland cypress (× Cupressocyparis leylandii) is a hardy, fast-growing hybrid between the Monterey cypress (Hesperocyparis macrocarpa) and the Nootka cypress (Callitropsis nootkatensis). Thriving in full sun to partial shade, it tolerates pollution, salt spray, and a variety of soils, including moist, well-drained, and clay soils. Commonly planted in gardens as a quick-growing boundary or shelter hedge, it is not widely used for forestry. Its adaptability and resilience make it a popular choice for landscaping, particularly in USDA hardiness zones 6 to 10.

Sensory-modality-specific primary afferents were molecularly defined into clusters using RT-PCR and receptor expression profiling. The study identified four sub-clusters: NP1-3 and TH. NP1-3 transmit itch, with NP1 responding to lysophosphatidic acid (Lpar3, Lpar5), NP2 to chloroquine (Mrgpra3, Mrgprx1), and NP3 to inflammatory itch via IL-31 (Il31ra, Osmr) and cysteine leukotrienes (Cysltr2). NP2 and NP3 also express histamine receptor Hrh1, while NP3 uniquely expresses serotonin receptors (Htr1f, Htr2a). TH is involved in pleasant touch, expressing VGLUT3 (Slc17a8). NP1-3 express Plcb3, with NP2 and NP3 co-expressing Trpv1, delimiting histaminergic itch to these clusters. Nociceptive information is transmitted by NP2 and NP3, while proprioceptive information is linked to ATOH-1 lineage neurons. This molecular diversity highlights distinct pathways for histaminergic and non-histaminergic itch signaling.

John Hart, a native of Winter Park, Florida, graduated from Winter Park High School before attending Seminole Junior College, where he played as a catcher on the baseball team. He began his professional career as a catcher with the Montreal Expos organization in 1969, playing three seasons. Hart later graduated from the University of Central Florida in 1973, majoring in history and physical education, earning All-American honors during his collegiate baseball career.

Lechenaultia is a genus of small woody or semi-woody plants endemic to Australia, belonging to the Goodeniaceae family. These plants typically have slender, spreading branches and leaves that vary in shape, often small and narrow. Their flowers have five sepals and vibrant petals in shades of blue, green, yellow, red, or white. The two rear petals are often smaller or differently shaped, while the lower three are fan-shaped. Lechenaultia produces dry, capsule-like fruits containing seeds.

Mick Connaboy was granted time by the SFA at the end of the season to consider his next move, and just 12 days later, he signed for Cowdenbeath. During the early months of the season, Connaboy showcased his versatility by occupying both midfield and defensive positions. In October, he scored his first goal of the season in a match against Falkirk. The game was notable for an unusual incident involving Cowdenbeath’s goalkeeper, who was forced to play outfield after an injury crisis left the team short of substitutes. Despite Connaboy’s contributions, Cowdenbeath took disciplinary action against him in March, citing a breach of club rules, which led to a temporary suspension.

In wake of the April 2004 referendum on unification, and the support of the Turkish Cypriot community for the plan, the European Union made pledges towards ending the isolation of northern Cyprus. The proposal for trade between the EU and the Turkish Cypriots was never implemented due to opposition from the Greek Cypriots, who argue that trade would amount to indirect recognition of the TRNC. Proposals to open up trade were discussed by Parliament again in 2010, but still opposed by the Republic of Cyprus.

AdultDVDEmpire.com received the Adult Video News (AVN) award for Best Retail Website in 2000 and won the same award in subsequent years, including 2001, 2002, 2003, and 2006. In 2015, the site was honored with the XBIZ Award for Retail Site of the Year and has also won the XBIZ Award for Adult Site of the Year (VOD) in 2014, 2015, and 2016. Jeff Rix and John-Michael D'Arcangelo were featured in AVN's July 2007 article, "50 Under 40: The Most Influential People In Porn," alongside representatives from other online retailers such as GameLink and Adam & Eve. The article highlighted three online retailers among the industry's most influential figures.

The Paul Bunyan Axe was created by the Wisconsin letterwinners' organization (the National W Club) and would be instituted as the trophy in the series in 1948. The scores of each game are recorded on the axe's handle, which is 6 feet long. A new axe was created in 2000. The original axe was donated to the College Football Hall of Fame in 2003.

John Farley was born in Newtownhamilton, County Armagh, Ireland, to Catherine (née Murphy) and Philip Farrelly. At age twelve, he was orphaned and went to live with his mother's family in the townland of Moyles. He received his early education under the direction of a private tutor named Hugh McGuire. He then attended St. Macartan's College in Monaghan from 1859 to 1864.

The Aston Hall Hospital site in Aston-on-Trent reveals a multi-phase prehistoric landscape, with evidence of activity spanning the Mesolithic to the Late Bronze Age. Early Neolithic pits across the site, containing Peterborough Ware and Grooved Ware in the Durrington Walls style (2900–2100 cal BC), demonstrate sustained human activity and evolving use of the landscape. Items such as lithics and pottery were uncovered in these pits, alongside evidence of the East Midlands variant of the Deverel-Rimbury ceramic tradition, dated to the Middle Bronze Age, with fabrics incorporating grog and organic materials. The site’s proximity to the Aston Cursus and Willington Cursus, along with associated funerary barrows, highlights its significance within a broader ceremonial and funerary landscape, typically attributed to the Neolithic and Early Bronze Age. These findings underscore the long-lived and dynamic prehistoric use of the River Trent’s middle valley, reflecting shifts in settlement, ritual, and material culture over millennia.

Cyrus Harris (1817-1888), was a native of Mississippi but moved to Mill Creek in 1855. He became the first governor of the Chickasaw Nation and was re-elected to the position four more times. He made his home near the present town of Mill Creek and was buried in Old Mill Creek Cemetery.

In October 1976, 27-year-old Randall Adams and his brother left Ohio for California. En route, they arrived in Dallas on Thanksgiving night. The next morning, Adams was offered a contracting job. On the following Saturday, November 27, Adams went to start work but no one turned up because it was a weekend. On the way home, his car ran out of fuel.

Ponil Complex Fire started in North County, above highway 64 from Dean Canyon area, and reached to the Valle Vidal area. By July 6, the fire had burned 60,000 acres, and there was no estimate of when the fire will be fully contained. It was upgraded to Type I incident. 1,342 firefighters, 13 water-dropping helicopters, 31 engines, 24 dozers, and 12 water tenders were fighting against the fire. By June 11, the fire had burned 85,000 acres of land. It was fully contained on June 17.

In 2007, Gioia voluntarily chose to live on $28 for one week, in order to bring attention to the daily struggle of millions of New Yorkers. He stated, "But as tough as this week has been for me, the sad fact is that it was nothing compared with what over 1.1 million New Yorkers face every day. Far too many New Yorkers make impossible choices among health care for their children, paying their rent or putting food on the table on a daily basis."

When the American Civil War broke out in April 1861, Robert F. R. Lewis was aboard the USS Sabine, serving as an officer during the Union blockade. Tasked with blockading the Gulf Coast, particularly near Sabine Pass, Lewis played a key role in enforcing the Union’s naval strategy. He also participated in carrying reinforcements to Fort Pickens, which faced a threat from the Confederate-held Fort McRee. Appointed as executive officer on the Sabine, he continued his service until the ship was decommissioned later in 1861. Subsequently, Lewis joined the USS De Soto in the West Gulf Blockading Squadron, where he served as executive officer. He was promoted to lieutenant commander in 1862, further advancing his naval career.

The Atoll Fruit-Dove (Ptilinopus coralensis) is the only dove in the tropical Pacific adapted exclusively to low coral atolls, where it resides in French Polynesia. It primarily feeds on insects and seeds, foraging in the canopy of native trees. These trees, notable for their adaptation to atoll environments, provide essential sustenance and habitat.

Peter Rouch was ordained as a deacon in the Church of England in 1999 and as a priest in 2000. He served his curacy from 1999 to 2002 at St John the Evangelist, East Dulwich, in the Diocese of Southwark. From 2002 to 2004, he was a Junior Research Fellow at St Stephen's House, Oxford, and during the 2003–2004 academic year, he served as chaplain to St John's College, Oxford. He completed his Doctor of Philosophy degree at the University of Manchester in 2005. His doctoral thesis focused on the theology of priesthood, exploring its implications for the Church's mission and ministry.

By the summer of 1977, three episodes of the first season of *Old Friends ... New Friends* had been filmed and were set to be shown to select audiences. The Richard King Mellon Foundation initially provided $250,000 for these episodes, with a total funding commitment of $500,000 for the entire first season. Fred Rogers referred to this as a "seed grant," emphasizing its foundational role. An additional $250,000 was later donated by the foundation for the remaining four episodes. Fred Rogers' biographer noted the insecurity of the funding, citing high production costs and limited sponsorship as factors that made the series financially unsustainable despite its creative ambition and educational value.

A bomb blast in front of a Malagutay district karaoke bar near a military arms depot in Zamboanga City killed an American Green Beret commando and three Filipino civilians. At least 25 other people, one of them another American trooper, were wounded in the blast. Investigators were looking at the possibility of a suicide attack as the rider of a motorcycle where the explosives were rigged was among the fatalities. A military intelligence report said the attack was staged by a "four-man urban terrorist group" of Abu Sayyaf, which has been linked to al-Qaeda. Philippine National Police chief Director General Hermogenes Ebdane Jr. stated the motorcycle driver had been identified as a member of Abu Sayyaf from a sketch based on witnesses' accounts and the body. According to police, the bomb that exploded in Malagutay weighed around a kilo and contained "brownish" explosive that may have been a mixture of gunpowder and TNT.

The race was created in 1947 by newspaper Le Dauphiné libéré to promote its circulation. After World War II, as cycling recovered from a universal five- or six-year hiatus, the Grenoble-based newspaper decided to create and organize a cycling stage race covering the Dauphiné region. The race was named after the newspaper and set in June, prior to the Tour de France. Polish rider Edward Klabiński won the inaugural edition.

Two days after the shooting, Oklahoma City police found Howell's Suburban parked near a convenience store on the south side of town. Officers canvassed the area to determine who left it there and spoke with Kermit Lottie, who owned an auto body shop four blocks from where the vehicle was found. Lottie told police that a man he identified as "Day Day" and another man whom he did not know had tried to sell him the Suburban the day before, but he refused to buy it. Lottie provided police with Day Day's business card, which bore his legal name, Ladell King, and his contact information.

The NCDOT I-4729 Feasibility Study was completed in 2006 and recommended improvements to NC 108 at its interchange with I-26 in Columbus, including widening the facility to four lanes. The project was split into two sections, A and B, with the former including modifications to the I-26 and US 74 interchange. Construction of Section A began in 2017 and was completed in 2019. Section B will improve NC 108, including the construction of a new structure across I-26, but will only add a center turn lane west of the interchange. Construction was scheduled to begin in 2021, but has been delayed until 2029.

At 10:00 a.m. on September 18, President of the United States George Washington and his entourage crossed the Potomac River to arrive in the city of Washington. There, they were joined by an escort consisting of the Alexandria Volunteer Artillery, and members of Masonic lodges from Virginia and Maryland, and proceeded to the construction grounds approximately 1.5 miles (2.4 km) away. At the site of the Capitol, Washington was received by Joseph Clark, the Grand Master of the Grand Lodge of Maryland. A silver plaque, fashioned by Georgetown silversmith Caleb Bentley, was handed to Washington, who stepped into the foundation trench and placed the plaque, whereupon the cornerstone was lowered. The plaque was inscribed with a brief tribute to the "military valor and prudence" of Washington, and dedicated the building in the "first year, of the second term, of the presidency of George Washington ... and in the year of Masonry 5,793."

AdultDVDEmpire.com has won Adult Video News' Best Retail Website Award in 2000, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2016, 2017, 2018 and 2020 as well as the XBIZ award for Adult Site of the Year (VOD) in 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2020. In addition to these awards, Jeff Rix and John-Michael D'Arcangelo were featured in the July 2007 issue of AVN, in an article entitled "50 Under 40: The Most Influential People In Porn". DVDEmpire.com was one of only four featured online retailers, along with Good Vibrations, Sportsheets International, Inc., and WantedList.

The museum has around 25 to 30 exhibitions per year. In 2021 and 2022 exhibitions have included Portugese painter Paula Rego, Basque fashion designer Cristóbal Balenciaga and English potter and artist Grayson Perry.  The 2021 exhibition Monet: The Garden Paintings was voted as the best museum exhibition in the Netherlands.

In 2015, Prioria vendor Condor Aerial alleged that Prioria was providing false specifications for the Maveric UAV, selling a hobby-grade drone at a military-grade price and selling refurbished drones as new. The complaint included allegations that the UAV was easily damaged and also consisted of sworn statements by a prior employee who claimed that then-CEO of Prioria was knowingly falsifying information on government contracts. A report by NOAA documented that their own Maveric drones lasted over six years without mechanical incident over the course of 4,500 combined sorties.

Racing Louisville FC shares Lynn Family Stadium with Louisville City FC. Opened in 2020, the stadium has a seated capacity of 11,600 and a total capacity of 15,304. The club's training facilities are located at Louisville Champions Park, a 40-acre site designed to support player development and team operations.

In February 2012, the online marketing blog LL Social broke the news that Pinterest had been using Skimlinks to monetize user-generated content. Skimlinks' software appended affiliate codes to links shared by users, allowing Pinterest to earn a percentage of revenue from purchases made through those links. The partnership, which had been ongoing quietly for an unspecified period, faced backlash when users discovered the covert nature of the monetization strategy. Public criticism centered on the lack of transparency, with many accusing Pinterest of profiting off users' content without disclosure. In response to the controversy, Pinterest CEO Ben Silbermann clarified that the use of Skimlinks was a test rather than a formal business plan. Pinterest ended its relationship with Skimlinks a week before the story broke, citing the negative response.

Letheby was born at Plymouth, England, in 1816, and studied chemistry at the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Society. In 1837 he commenced the study of medicine and became the assistant of Jonathan Pereira. He graduated M.B. at the University of London in 1842, and was also LSA (Licentiate of the Society of Apothecaries) (1837) and PhD.

An August 17, 1991 article entitled "Judge Inflames Aids Case" states: "U.S. District Judge Robert G. Doumar said that people infected with the AIDS virus 'should be shot' if they have unprotected sex." In 2017, Doumar stated from the bench that child porn producers should be "shot." In an interview with The Virginian-Pilot, he confirmed "I said it. I said that they should be shot."

Steve Winwood left Traffic in 1974 due to health issues, including peritonitis, and retreated to session work before releasing his self-titled solo debut in 1977, which was initially poorly received. His 1980 album *Arc of a Diver* featured his first solo hit, while 1982's *Talking Back to the Night* continued his creative resurgence, showcasing his mastery of writing, performing, and producing.

By 1977, three episodes of the first season were filmed and were to be shown to sponsorship candidates that summer. The Richard King Mellon Foundation gave $250,000 for the first three episodes and agreed to provide a total funding of $1 million under what Rogers called "a kind of matching grant", with the remaining required funding having to be received from other donors. The foundation donated an additional $250,000 to fund the remaining four episodes of the first season. The foundation later granted the $1 million total. According to Maxwell King, a biographer of Rogers, the funding was insecure. This, along with low ratings, made the series' production unsustainable.
Ofentse Mwase, born in Rustenburg, South Africa, developed an interest in filmmaking early on. In 2010, he shot the short film *iGolide*, earning an AFDA Best Cinematographer nomination.

Charles Cathcart, 8th Lord Cathcart, was born in 1686 and became a major in the Scots Greys in 1709. During the Jacobite rising of 1715, he commanded a detachment of dragoons, defeating the Jacobites at the Skirmish of Dunfermline in October 1715. His troops later outflanked the Jacobite forces at the Battle of Sheriffmuir, playing a key role in the conflict.

As of March 2007, there were 13 Jewish baseball players in the major leagues, including Scott Feldman and his Jewish teammate Ian Kinsler. Feldman was featured in the 2008 Hank Greenberg 75th Anniversary edition of Jewish Major Leaguers Baseball Cards, commemorating Jewish players from 1871 through 2008, alongside notable names such as Ryan Braun, Brad Ausmus, Kevin Youkilis, Gabe Kapler, Jason Marquis, and Craig Breslow. In 2009, Feldman was named the Jewish Major Leaguers Pitcher of the Year after achieving 17 wins, the most by a Jewish pitcher since Steve Stone’s 25-win season in 1980. Through 2017, Feldman ranked 6th among Jewish major leaguers in career strikeouts and 8th in career wins, solidifying his place as one of the most accomplished Jewish pitchers in modern baseball history.

Campos Seijo began her publishing career in the UK as a technical editor at the European Respiratory Society. Her first Editor-in-Chief assignment came as head of a suite of pharmaceutical technology titles at Advanstar Communications, now part of UBM. In 2009, she became Editor-in-Chief of Chemistry World, the monthly news magazine published by the Royal Society of Chemistry. In 2014 she took on her current role at C&EN and moved to Washington, DC.

Weariness with the grind of touring and recording prompted Winwood to leave Traffic and retire to sessioning for some years. Under pressure from Island Records, he resurfaced with his self-titled first solo album in 1977. This was followed by his 1980 hit Arc of a Diver (which included his first solo hit, "While You See a Chance") and Talking Back to the Night in 1982.

In January 2012, Ryan Hall ran a 2:09:30 at the Olympic marathon trials in Houston, Texas, finishing second behind Meb Keflezighi. This performance secured Hall a spot on his second Olympic team for the London 2012 Games. However, during the Olympic marathon, Hall was forced to drop out around mile 11 due to hamstring tightness. Following the Olympics, Hall signed up for the New York City Marathon but later withdrew because of ongoing injury issues, continuing a challenging period marked by physical setbacks.

As of the 2004/09 survey, Küssnacht covers a total area of approximately 36.2 square kilometers. Of this, 47.6% is used for agricultural purposes, 31.6% is forested, 8.2% is settled with buildings or roads, and 12.6% is considered unproductive land. The area covered with buildings totaled 297 hectares, marking an increase of 72 hectares since 1982. Over the same period, recreational space in Küssnacht expanded by 9 hectares.

During the early 1990s, Scott was a part-time announcer with More FM. He was the 2003 sales person of the year of the NZ Radio Awards. In 2004, as promotions advisor for RadioWorks, Scott awarded The Edge 97.5 Wellington/WelTec Scholarship to a recipient student of the Wellington Institute of Technology.

William Paterson was originally commissioned as an ensign in the 98th Regiment of Foot, serving in India, where he collected botanical specimens and corresponded with Sir Joseph Banks. After the regiment's disbandment in 1787, he transferred to the 73rd Regiment of Foot. Promoted to captain in the New South Wales Corps in 1789 under Major Francis Grose, he arrived in Sydney in October 1791. From November 1791 to March 1793, Paterson commanded on Norfolk Island, collecting botanical specimens, including seeds sent to Banks and nurseries such as Lee and Kennedy and Colvill. In April 1798, he was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society for his contributions to natural history.
NHPCC, is located in the Ayazağa Campus. It is the national center for high performance computing. The super computer of this center was one of the world's top 500 list super computers (240th).

The town of Kanyakumari, located at the southernmost tip of Tamil Nadu, is associated with the goddess Devi Kanya Kumari. Linked to the mythical lost continent of Kumari Kandam, she is revered as the goddess who killed the demon Banasura, who had performed penance with utmost austerities. According to Vaishnava saint Vadiraja Tirtha in his *Tirtha Prabandha*, Kanya Kumari is a form of Lakshmi who descended to earth to slay Banasura and restore balance.

Born in Berlin-Charlottenburg, Hülper received vocal training at the Berlin University of the Arts. Afterwards she attended the acting school of the Oldenburgisches Staatstheater. She gained her first stage experience from 1938, also at the Oldenburg State Theatre, as a singer in the chorus. From 1939 to 1942, she was engaged as a soloist at the Staatstheater Cottbus. Stations at the Stadttheater Fürth followed and from 1945 onwards at various stages in Berlin Hebbel Theater, Metropol-Theater, Metropole and Deutsche Oper Berlin). Her repertoire covered the entire classical and modern operetta repertoire. She appeared in Franz Lehár's Paganini, Jacques Offenbach's La Vie parisienne, Gräfin Dubarry by Carl Millöcker and Theo Mackeben and Wiener Blut by Johann Strauss II. In 1967, she ended her stage career.

The recording was made at New York's The Town Hall at 8:30 P.M. on Saturday, April 24, 1965. Here, for the first time, the professor discussed his imagined studies of the life and "recently discovered" works of P. D. Q. Bach. Some of New York's finest musicians were on hand to give splendid performances, according to Howard Klein of The New York Times. Klein, realizing this was a one-time concert, hoped that a suitable foundation could be found to fund a nationwide tour.

Colt Lyerla enrolled at the University of Oregon in the spring of 2011, choosing the school to stay close to his Hillsboro community. During the spring game, he caught three passes for 37 yards. As a true freshman tight end, Lyerla recorded seven catches for 147 yards and five touchdowns during the 2011 season.

A music video for the song was released to Belly's YouTube channel alongside the song on July 22, 2021. The "glossy, action-packed music video" was directed by Christian Breslauer. Belly is burning along with some buildings that are on fire, The Weeknd drives a BMW and is seen later standing with McLaren P1 drifting behind him while Young Thug is seen with a slime green tank truck.

16S ribosomal RNA is the most common housekeeping genetic marker for classifying and identifying bacterial species, as it is present in all bacterial species, has an identical function in most organisms, and is large enough (~1,500 bp) to capture sufficient variation to distinguish bacteria. The sequence of 16S rRNA consists of highly conserved sequences which alternate with nine windows of "hypervariable regions". This allows universal primers to be used to sequence many species at a time, and provides the possibility of distinguishing bacteria given the variable regions alone. Many papers suggest that 16S rRNA gene sequencing provides genus identification in >90% of cases, but species level identification in approximately ~65 to 83% of cases. The Ribosomal Database Project (RDP) and SILVA databases contain sequence information for rRNA in bacteria, eukarya and archaea.

On January 19, 1993, an F-4G Wild Weasel fired an AGM-88 HARM at an Iraqi SAM site after a 14-nautical-mile lock-on east of Mosul. An hour later, an F-16C was fired upon by AAA but was not hit. Two hours later, a section of F-16Cs responded by dropping cluster bombs on anti-aircraft guns north of Mosul. Iraq declared a ceasefire on January 20 to celebrate President Clinton's inauguration.

The Chūnichi Shinpo, a biweekly paper, is published in Chinese and Japanese. The Chūbun and Zhongwen Dabao, both weekly newspapers, and about 28 other Chinese newspapers are published in Tokyo. In addition the Kansai Kabun Jihō, published in Chinese and Japanese, is based in the Osaka area.

During Operation Desert Storm in 1991, the U.S. Air Force deployed over 240 F-16s, which flew more sorties than any other aircraft, accounting for 25% of all missions. These sorties targeted airfields, radar systems, Scud missile sites, tanks, and other military assets. F-16s typically carried a range of munitions, including Mk 84 and Mk 82 bombs, CBU-52 and CBU-58 cluster munitions, CBU-87 Combined Effects Munitions, and AGM-65 Maverick missiles. The 388th Tactical Fighter Wing played a key role, with its F-16Ds serving as spotter aircraft to identify and mark targets for strike missions. These F-16Ds were equipped with LANTIRN pods for night operations and precision targeting. During the conflict, seven F-16s were lost in combat operations between January 16 and February 28, with three confirmed as downed by enemy action. Despite these losses, the F-16’s versatility and effectiveness were critical to the success of the air campaign, which significantly weakened Iraqi military capabilities.

In March 2005, Guylian was awarded a Guinness World Record for constructing the largest chocolate Easter egg sculpture. Built to celebrate Easter traditions and the renovation of the market square in Sint-Niklaas, Belgium, the egg was supported by metal scaffolding and wooden panels. Twenty-six Guylian chocolatiers worked for eight days to create the sculpture, using 1,950 kilograms (4,300 lbs) of chocolate, equivalent to around 50,000 Seashell Bars. The egg measured 8.32 meters (27.3 ft) tall and 6.39 meters (21 ft) wide, holding the record until 2011 when a larger egg was built in Italy.

The National ABU Robocon 2017 took place in the Shri Shiv Chhatrapati Krida Sankul, Pune on 2–4 March 2017. The contest theme has been declared as "Asobi : The Landing Disc”. College of Engineering, Pune won the National ABU Robocon 2017 Contest. Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Pune, was the first runner-up.

The FM-1 and FM-2 Wildcats were variants of the Grumman F4F Wildcat produced by General Motors (GM) under its Eastern Aircraft division after Grumman ceased Wildcat production in early 1943 to focus on the newer F6F Hellcat. GM produced a total of 5,280 FM variants, which were used by both the U.S. Navy and the British Fleet Air Arm. While the Wildcat was slower than the F6F Hellcat (380 mph/610 km/h) and the F4U Corsair (446 mph/718 km/h), it remained adequate for operations aboard smaller escort carriers late in the war. These carriers, which typically carried only two types of aircraft—the Wildcat and the GM-built TBM Avenger—relied on the Wildcat's lower landing speed and ability to take off without a catapult, making it well-suited for their shorter flight decks. GM initially produced the FM-1, which was nearly identical to the F4F-4 but reduced the armament to four .50 caliber M2 Browning machine guns and added wing racks for two 250 lb bombs or six rockets. The improved FM-2, based on Grumman's XF4F-8 prototype, featured a more powerful 1,350 hp Wright R-1820-56 engine, a taller tail to handle increased torque, reduced weight, and better maneuverability, making it the best-performing Wildcat variant.

At forward, Kassian began the year on the top line with the Sedin twins. Kassian scored five goals in the first seven games of the season. Partway through the Canucks' second game of February, Kassian was replaced on the top line by Alexandre Burrows. Kassian was dropped to the third line. Despite Kassian surpassing his goal total from his rookie season, the Sedins started the year with their lowest output in nearly ten years, combining for 14 points in the first 10 games. Coinciding with the line changes, Vancouver went on a six-game winning streak between the end of January and their February 12 game. After the last game of their winning streak, the Canucks were expecting Kesler to return to the line-up.

On March 10, Andhra state government started a two-day door-to door survey to identify people who had recently traveled to COVID-19 affected countries. This was carried out through a network of ASHA workers, Auxiliary nurse midwife (ANM), village and ward volunteers. Until 19 April, 32,000 people were identified with influenza like symptoms in the process.

In France, "L'Autre Finistère" debuted at number 22 on the chart edition of 27 February 1993, which was the highest debut that week; however, it dropped the two weeks later until reaching number 39, but climbed the following weeks and eventually peaked at number ten for a sole week in its 11th week. It remained for 17 weeks in the top 50, nine of them in the top 20. On the European Hot 100, it started at number 100 on 3 April 1993, reached a peak of number 54 in its eighth week and fell off the chart after ten weeks of presence.

In 2008, *Santogold* was widely acclaimed and featured on numerous year-end lists. Billboard ranked it as the 6th best album of the year, while it also secured the 6th spot on year-end lists by *NME* and *Slant Magazine*. The album placed 7th on the *Pazz & Jop* critics' poll and was highlighted by publications such as *The Guardian*, *Pitchfork*, and *Rolling Stone*. Its genre-blending sound and innovative approach earned it recognition as one of the standout releases of the year, solidifying its place in the broader cultural and critical conversation of the late 2000s.

In 2019, Anton Bakov published the book *The State is You!*, where he detailed his efforts to establish a physical territory for the Romanov Empire through the construction of artificial islands. The book revealed that officials initially responded to his proposals with enthusiasm, accepting funds and promising support, but later abandoned the projects without clear reasons. Bakov suggested that unknown forces were working against him. His seven-year campaign in The Gambia included negotiations with the government, during which President Yahya Jammeh granted him permission to conduct oceanic measurements for the construction of artificial islands. The engineering draft for the project was led by Bakov’s team, but after a regime change in The Gambia in 2016, further talks failed. Following these setbacks, Bakov decided to relocate the project to neutral Mediterranean waters near Venice, renaming it *Arca Noë*. This new initiative aligns with the seasteading movement, which seeks to create sustainable, autonomous communities at sea. Bakov plans to convert former supporters of the Romanov Empire project into members of *Arca Noë*. He announced that the project would commence after the coronavirus pandemic, marking a new chapter in his vision for an alternative state.

Satannish is a demonic being of pure mystical energy, an embodiment of evil. The guise he most often employs around human sorcerers is that of a green-skinned, horned being with a second face in his stomach. Satannish is more akin to a supernatural force of nature than a being; as such he possesses "virtually unlimited" mystic power which can be used for a variety of effects, including (but not limited to) inter-dimensional teleportation, manipulation of time, space, and matter, size transformations, casting bolts of mystic energy as destructive force, demonic possession, and physical strength and durability. The character's extent of power has been shown as equal to that of Mephisto. Satannish's ability to manifest in the dimension of Earth is, in some unknown way, limited by mystic factors and he frequently makes use of pawns native to Earth for his purposes. He also proved to be powerful enough to easily kill all the invading Skrulls who attempted to take Avalon's magic, which no other magical being in Avalon was able to do.

During the punishment phase of Julius Jones' trial, testimony was presented regarding a robbery that occurred after dinner at the Hideaway Pizza on July 22, 1999. Anand Lapsi testified that he went to his GMC Suburban in the parking lot that night when a man wearing a white T-shirt and a red bandana held a gun to his head, demanding, "Give me the keys." Lapsi identified Julius Jones as the robber. Police later found the Suburban in the parking lot of Jones' apartment, missing its stereo. The keys to the vehicle were located inside Jones' apartment during a subsequent search.

The Ponil Complex Fire, ignited by lightning strikes on June 3, 2002, started in Dean Canyon near US Route 64 in northern New Mexico. By June 6, it had burned 60,000 acres and was upgraded to a Type I incident, with no containment timeline. By June 11, the fire had scorched over 92,000 acres. It involved hundreds of firefighters and multiple water-dropping helicopters. The fire was fully contained on June 17, 2002, after causing significant ecological damage, including the loss of four fish species and widespread forest destruction.

Moreno redshirted in 2006, and entered the 2007 season as a redshirt freshman under head coach Mark Richt. After a great spring, Moreno shared time with Thomas Brown as the starting running back in the season opener against Oklahoma State. Moreno totaled 70 yards rushing on 12 carries with two receptions for 51 yards in the 35–14 win. In the following game against South Carolina, Moreno saw most of the rushing duties with 14 carries for 104 yards in the 12–16 loss. In the third game of the season against Western Carolina, Moreno scored his first career college touchdown in a 45–16 win over Western Carolina. His totals on the day were 94 yards on 13 rushes with one touchdown.

After Muhammad's death, his son, Hussein Tawfiq al-Assad, took control of the criminal network and its armed contingent. He went on to rebrand and reorganize it as official paramilitary unit, and around late June/early July 2015 officially "founded" the "Lions of Hussein Brigade". According to regional expert Aymenn Jawad Al-Tamimi, the new name of the group could be understood as referring both to Hussein Tawfiq al-Assad himself as well as Imam Husayn ibn Ali. The reorganization aimed at reducing the group's previous negative image that had resulted from its association with Muhammad's criminal activities. According to 2017 reports by the Austrian Armed Forces' Truppendienst magazine and a Clingendael Institute researcher the militia became part of the Russian-backed and Republican Guard-led 4th Corps. A commander of the Lions of Hussein later denied any affiliation with the 4th Corps or Russia. The Lions of Hussein consequently fought for the government in various war zones, participating in the Palmyra offensive (July–August 2015) against the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL), the Al-Ghab offensive (July–August 2015), the offensive to recapture Marj al-Sultan airbase in late 2015, and the 2015–16 Latakia offensive. Meanwhile, the unit reportedly continues its "criminal activities on the side".

Pierre Collingwood Donat was born in Kentville, Nova Scotia, Canada, the son of Marie (née Bardet) and Philip Ernst Donat, a landscape gardener. Richard Donat, who starred on the television show Haven, is Peter's younger brother. His uncle was Oscar winning British actor Robert Donat. Peter Donat emigrated to the United States in 1950, studied drama at Yale University, and first came to attention as a stage actor in the lead of a production of Cyrano de Bergerac. In 1961, he played a leading role in Donald Jack's stage play The Canvas Barricade, the first Canadian play performed at the Stratford Festival.
Ibrahim Kanapacki died on October 7, 2005. Newspapers, including *Zikr ul'-Kitab*, paid tribute to him. In 2006, a plaque was placed at his family home in Smilavichy, Belarus.

Pūrerehua are traditional Māori wind instruments, typically elliptical in shape and made from materials such as wood, bone, or pounamu (New Zealand greenstone). Attached to a long cord, they are swung in a circular motion to produce a whirring sound, with pitch varying by speed. Pūrerehua are used for communication, carrying the player’s words and dreams to both physical and spiritual realms.

The Department of Correctional Services (DCS) in Jamaica, under the Ministry of National Security, focuses on offender rehabilitation and reintegration while ensuring public safety through secure custody. Led by Commissioner of Corrections Brigadier Radgh Mason, reporting to the Permanent Secretary, DCS collaborates with stakeholders like Pearnel Patroe Charles Jr. to enhance rehabilitation programmes aimed at reducing recidivism. Charles has prioritized improving these initiatives while maintaining security and community trust since his appointment.

The RECLAIM Act amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act (SMCRA), which was enacted in 1977 to address environmental degradation from coal mining and promote land restoration. The SMCRA established the Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE) within the Department of the Interior. OSMRE is responsible for regulating surface coal mining, reclaiming abandoned mine sites, and protecting communities and the environment from mining impacts, while also supporting economic development through land restoration and cleanup efforts.

Wilson was made a deacon at Petertide (1 July) 1979 by Eric Wild, Bishop of Reading, at St Peter's, Didcot, and ordained a priest the Petertide following (29 June 1980) by Patrick Rodger, Bishop of Oxford, at Christ Church Cathedral, Oxford. From 1979 to 1981 he was an honorary assistant curate of Eynsham (where he met his future wife) and also held an academic position funded by the University of Oxford. From 1981 to 1982, he was an assistant curate in the same parish, his academic position having ended.

The University of Illinois Willard Airport was dedicated on October 26, 1945, with airline flights beginning in 1954. The original terminal, built in 1960, was replaced by the current facility in 1987. By 1969, it was the second-busiest airport in Illinois. However, the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978 led airlines to reduce service to smaller airports like Willard, causing a decline in passenger numbers. The growing airfare price gap with major hubs like Chicago O’Hare further diminished demand at Willard.

In January 2012, Hall ran a 2:09:30 at the Olympic marathon trials in Houston, Texas. Although leading at the halfway point, Hall eventually finished second behind Meb Keflezighi, still securing a spot on his second Olympic team. At the Olympics in London, Hall dropped out of the marathon around the eleven mile mark while he ran this race with a hamstring injury. After his disappointment at the Olympics, Hall signed up for the New York City Marathon for later that year, but was unable to run it and withdrew from the race (before it was cancelled due to Hurricane Sandy) in September.

Mutukula is located in extreme southern Kyotera District at the international border between Uganda and Tanzania. The town is approximately 46 kilometres (29 mi), south of Kyotera, where the district headquarters are located. This is about 91 kilometres (57 mi), by road, south-west of Masaka, the nearest large city. This location is approximately 224 kilometres (139 mi), by road, south-west of Kampala, the capital and largest city of Uganda. The coordinates of the town are 1°00'00.0"S, 31°25'00.0"E Latitude:-1.0000; Longitude:31.4167).

In 1960, the barque Skomvær was given a new lease of life through a successful fundraising campaign, with Erik Bye playing a pivotal role. Bye, a renowned Norwegian journalist, singer, and television host, released his debut solo album *Vi går ombord* the same year, featuring the sixth track, *Skomværsvalsen*. Co-written by Bye and composer Bjarne Amdahl, the song became a tribute to Norwegian seafarers and was heavily featured on Bye's popular radio show *Vi går ombord*. The song, with its opening line "Og jinta sa tel sonnavind," was also performed annually by the Norwegian Seamen's Choir during the Christmas Eve broadcast of the TV version of *Vi går ombord*, cementing its place in Norwegian maritime and cultural history.

On October 2, 2002, a bomb blast occurred in the Malagutay district of Zamboanga City, killing a U.S. soldier and two Filipino civilians. The explosion wounded at least 23 others, including Filipino civilians and American military personnel. Investigators reported that the bomb, which was stashed in a motorcycle, was detonated using a cellphone. Police stated that the device contained TNT and nails, designed to maximize casualties. Military intelligence identified the driver of the motorcycle, based on witness accounts, as a member of the Abu Sayyaf group, a terrorist organization linked to al-Qaeda. The group, known for its history of bombings and kidnappings, was suspected of orchestrating the attack.

He has criticised the left-green dominated German media for stifling debate about Islam in Germany, leading to ordinary people being afraid to state their opinions. As an example he gives Uwe Tellkamp, who expressed criticism against the German policy of migration and was attacked in mainstream media and painted as a right-extremist. He has also criticised authorities in Germany for not standing up to the large organised Islamic community organisations like the Turkish-Islamic Union for Religious Affairs and for not supporting liberal Muslims like Seyran Ateş and Necla Kelek.

In December 2013, Coleg y Cymoedd announced plans to build a new £20 million campus in Aberdare, replacing the existing facility in Cwmdare. Located on a 2.7-acre site at the former railway goods yard in Robertstown, the campus was designed to serve up to 800 learners and incorporate industry-standard facilities, including a professional hairdressing salon, restaurant, and coffee shop. Completed in 2017, the project received £11 million (55%) of its funding from the Welsh Government under the 21st Century Schools programme.

ABC News Now was a 24-hour digital cable news network launched by ABC News on March 2003, initially as a streaming news channel. It was unrelated to the earlier Satellite News Channel, which ceased operations in 1983. ABC News Now was available across multiple platforms, including cable, satellite, broadband, IPTV, and wireless, delivering live breaking news, hourly briefs, original reports, and programming from ABC News shows like "World News Tonight" and "Good Morning America." The service also featured user-generated content, allowing viewers to submit videos and personal thoughts. Available in the United States and internationally, it reached millions of users. ABC News Now ceased operations as a digital subchannel in 2005 but continued on other platforms until October 28, 2013, when it was replaced on cable providers by Fusion, a joint venture between ABC News and Univision.

Mazumdar was born on 5 January 1981, into a family of Majumdars in Ramganj, Noakhali District (now in Lakshmipur District), Bangladesh. Her father, Abdul Mannan Majumdar, was a businessman. Nishat was the second out of the four children of Abdul Mannan Majumdar and Ashura Majumdar. She received both bachelor's and master's degrees in accounting from Dhaka City College. Nishat is married to Rafikul Islam, a software engineer in a software firm.

The Helmand River, the longest river in Afghanistan at 1,150 km, originates in the Hindu Kush and flows southwestward, ending in the Hamun-i-Helmand in Iran's Sistan and Baluchistan province. One of its primary arms crosses through Lashkargah, giving the city a picturesque riverside charm. The river provides a popular setting for picnics and supports water sports such as swimming and boating, with boats available for public rental. Mirwais Nika Park was recently built on its banks, enhancing the recreational appeal. On the opposite side of the river lies a dense thicket, home to diverse trees and various species of birds, mammals, and reptiles, adding to the natural beauty and ecological significance of the area.

The recording of *Peter Schickele Presents an Evening with P. D. Q. Bach (1807–1742)?* was made live at Town Hall in New York City on December 27, 1965. During the concert, Peter Schickele humorously introduced his imagined studies of the life and works of P. D. Q. Bach. The event featured performances by Schickele and other musicians. Howard Klein, representing *The New York Times*, attended and hoped the concert would not be a one-time event, recognizing its unique comedic and musical appeal.

Krossfonn reached Lorient, German-occupied France, on 7 July 1940 and was renamed Spichern. Converted into a supply ship, Spichern bunkered Prinz Eugen with 764 tons of heavy fuel oil on 26 May 1941 during Operation Rheinübung.

His mother, Daryl Reich Rubenstein, was an art historian and curator at the Smithsonian institution. Rubenstein grew up in Washington, DC, attending Sidwell Friends School. He spent much of his childhood visiting museums in the United States and Europe. Led by his father, Lee Rubenstein, the family gained a reputation for creating large snow sculptures. Rubenstein spent much of his childhood creating projects with his hands, such as motorized go-carts. He also enjoyed sailing and canoeing, which he continues to this day. This has had a powering impact on his art.

The Aston Hall Hospital site displays evidence of a multi-phase prehistoric landscape which spans the Mesolithic through to the Late Iron Age; Sherds of undecorated, carinated bowl tradition pottery dating to the Early Neolithic, Grooved Ware of Clacton style (in use between 2900 cal BC and 2100 cal BC) and Flints dating to the Early Neolithic. Pits contained hazelnuts, mollusc shell, charcoal, spelt and rye grains. An East Midlands variant of the Deverel-Rimbury ceramic tradition, currently dated to the Middle Bronze Age were found to have similar fabrics containing rounded quartz sand and clastic sedimentary rock fragments as temper. Dating evidence from the [nearby] Aston Cursus, Willington Cursus, and associated earlier and later funerary barrows have normally been attributed to the Middle and Later Neolithic, however, the Early Neolithic pits across the site, although infrequent, demonstrate human activity in this landscape as early as the 4th millennium BC.

Beng Climaco graduated from Parsippany Hills High School in Parsippany-Troy Hills, New Jersey, United States. She earned a Bachelor of Science in Education from Ateneo de Zamboanga University and a master’s degree in family counseling from Ateneo de Manila University. At Ateneo de Zamboanga, she taught English and Religion and served as a guidance counselor.

Katakwi is located approximately 52 kilometers (32 miles) by road northeast of Soroti, the largest city in the Teso sub-region. It is about 153 kilometers (95 miles) by road northwest of Mbale, the largest city in Uganda's Eastern Region, and approximately 343 kilometers (213 miles) by road northeast of Kampala, the capital and largest city of Uganda. The town's coordinates are 01°54'54.0"N, 33°57'18.0"E (Latitude: 1.9150; Longitude: 33.9550).

Harry Davenport married actress Alice Shepard in 1893, and they had one daughter, Dorothy Davenport, who became a prominent actress. The couple divorced in 1896, the same year Harry married actress Phyllis Rankin. Together, Harry and Phyllis had three biological children—Kate, Ned, and Ann—all of whom became actors. Harry also adopted Phyllis's son from a previous marriage, Arthur Rankin, who was the father of Arthur Rankin Jr., co-founder of the Rankin/Bass animation studio. Actress Anne Seymour was Harry Davenport's great-niece through her mother, May Davenport, making her part of the extended Davenport family legacy in the entertainment industry.

While the 2009 season was strong on the field, club owner Tom Hicks became the focus of several reports indicating serious financial problems with his holding group, Hicks Sports Group, which also owned the Dallas Stars, the Frisco RoughRiders (the Rangers AA-farm club), 1⁄2 of Liverpool F.C. (sold in mid-October 2010 to New England Sports Ventures, owners of the Boston Red Sox), and the Mesquite Championship Rodeo (later sold by HSG). HSG was reported to have gone into default on a $525 million loan.

The area now known as Pascoe Vale South was originally part of the property 'Pascoeville,' owned by John Pascoe Fawkner. In 1841, Dr Farquhar McCrae, a Scottish-born surgeon, purchased land in the area and named it 'Moreland.' In 1842, he built Wentworth House (formerly La Rose) at 22 Le Cateau Street, Pascoe Vale South. Wentworth House is one of the oldest surviving stone houses in Victoria, showcasing early colonial building techniques and holding architectural and historical significance to the state.

After the election of King Haakon VII in 1905, the King and Queen Maud lived their first year in Norway at the estate while the Royal Palace in Oslo was refurbished. Queen Maud created new English landscape and rock gardens on the estate grounds with the help of Charles Edward Hubbard. The royal family would later use the estate as a summer residence, a tradition which was continued by King Olav V and later King Harald V.

Kotaro Umeda was born in Tokyo, Japan, and moved with his family to the United States at the age of six. Spotted by coach Kia Zolgharnain during his youth, Umeda attended Mayfield High School in Ohio. He later played NCAA Division I soccer at the University of Louisville and the University of Akron.

Spectrum Culture included the song on a list of Dylan's 20 best songs of the 2000s In an article accompanying the list, critic Jacob Nierenberg noted that "Sugar Baby" is "indebted to folk and blues without wholly belonging to either" genre, resulting in a "song that feels like a new American standard, an object that’s at once familiar and novel. 'You went years without me', Dylan harrumphs on the chorus, 'Might as well keep going now'. He seems to be singing it as much for himself as for the object of his affections".

The goddess is inextricably tied to the eponymous town of Kanyakumari, situated at the southernmost tip of Tamil Nadu. The worship of Devi Kanya Kumari is also associated with the Kumari Kandam, a mythical lost continent. Kanya Kumari is regarded to be the goddess who killed the demon Banasura, who performed a continuous penance with utmost austerities. The Shaivism Saint Vadiraja Tirtha, in his Tirtha Prabhanda, describes Kanya Kumari as Parvati, who descended upon the earth to slay Banasura.

According to the 2000 census, Coffrane had 25,992 Roman Catholics, making up 80.2% of the population, while 7.0% (2,255 individuals) belonged to the Swiss Reformed Church. There were 464 members of an Orthodox church (1.43%) and 497 individuals (1.53%) who belonged to another Christian church. Additionally, 985 individuals (3.04%) were Islamic. A total of 1,212 people (3.74%) belonged to no church, were agnostic, or atheist. Furthermore, 1,109 individuals (3.42% of the population) did not answer the question about religion.

In May 2019, Clyde Chambliss sponsored the Human Life Protection Act, which bans nearly all abortions in Alabama except when necessary to prevent a serious health risk to the mother. The law does not allow exceptions for cases of rape or incest and reclassifies abortion as a Class A felony, punishable by up to 99 years in prison for doctors. Chambliss defended the law, stating, "When God creates the miracle of life inside a woman's womb, it is not our place as human beings to extinguish that life."

In December 2013, Chris Cairns, former New Zealand cricket captain, was named in an ICC investigation into alleged match-fixing in the Indian Cricket League (ICL). Cairns denied the allegations, rejecting claims he attempted to recruit teammates in India.

Gerhard Bast, born into a German nationalist household, was the son of Rudolf Bast, a lawyer and member of the NSDAP. In 1912, the family moved to Amstetten, where Rudolf established a successful law practice. Gerhard attended high school in Linz before studying law at the University of Graz, where he joined the student fraternity "Germania." He graduated with a doctorate in law in 1931, the same year he joined the NSDAP (membership number 611,179). He became a member of the SS in 1932 (membership number 46,204).
