After graduating, Bast worked at the county court in St. Pölten but lost his position due to his political affiliations. He then worked at his father’s law firm. Rudolf Bast, like his son, was a committed National Socialist, which heavily influenced Gerhard’s ideological path.

Having qualified for his very first PBA Tournament of Champions, Svensson won the event held in Shawnee, Oklahoma on February 7, 2016. In doing so at age 20, he became the youngest-ever winner in this PBA major tournament, which dates back to 1962. Marshall Holman had previously held this distinction when he won the 1976 Tournament of Champions at age 21. Svensson won two more PBA titles in 2016, at the Brunswick Euro Challenge (Munich) and the World Bowling Tour Thailand event (Bangkok), giving him five PBA titles overall.

The Guinness World Record for the largest chocolate Easter egg sculpture was awarded to Guylian in March 2005. In celebration of Easter traditions and the renovation of Sint-Niklaas market square the record-breaking egg was constructed with support from metal scaffolding and wooden panels. Twenty-six Guylian chocolatiers built the egg over eight days using around 50,000 Seashell Bars worth of Guylian chocolate totalling 1,950 kilograms (4,300 lb). The sculpture reached 8.32 metres (27.3 ft) tall and 6.39 metres (21.0 ft) wide and held the record until 2011 when a 10 metres (33 ft) tall egg was built in Italy.

Reviews of The Joy Luck Club were generally positive. The film holds an 86% rating on Rotten Tomatoes based on 83 reviews, including 71 "fresh" ones. The site's consensus states: "The Joy Luck Club traces the generational divide, unearthing universal truths while exploring lives through the lens of a specific cultural experience." CinemaScore reported that audiences gave the film an "A+" grade. Critic Gene Siskel, singled out the script and performances, praising the film for presenting images of Asian-Americans outside the narrow range of childhood violinists and spelling bee winners, opining that its main accomplishments were its depiction of how the brutality of the lives of women in China could continue to influence the lives of their American daughters, and its ability to allow audiences to relate to a large group of Chinese-Americans as individuals. Siskel picked it as the seventh of the top ten movies of 1993, while Roger Ebert picked it as the fifth of his own top ten movies of 1993.

Santogold was ranked among the best albums of 2008 by numerous publications. Billboard named it the second best album of 2008, while Rolling Stone, Spin, and New York all listed the album at number 6 on their year-end lists. Pazz & Jop ranked it at number 7 on their annual list. NME also ranked it at number 7, while Slant Magazine placed it at number 9. Other publications who featured Santogold on their year-end lists include Q, The Guardian, Pitchfork, Consequence of Sound, Paste, PopMatters, Drowned in Sound, Gigwise, Mixmag, musicOMH, Blender, and more.

Interrupted only by the ducal exile, Sala gave lectures on chemistry at the University of Rostock where Johann Rist was one of Sala's students. Peter Lauremberg, also a Paracelsianist at Rostock wrote a discussion of Sala's ideas. Lauremberg's polite questioning was answered polemically by Sala's later son-in-law Anton Günther Billich which escalated the dispute.

Anutin Charnvirakul entered politics in 1996 as an advisor to Foreign Affairs Minister Prachuab Chaiyasan. He served as Deputy Minister of Public Health from July to October 2004 and March 2005 to September 2006, and as Deputy Minister of Commerce from October 2004 to March 2005. Following the dissolution of the Thai Rak Thai Party in 2007, he was banned from politics for five years. Anutin joined the Bhumjaithai Party in 2012 and became its leader on November 14, 2012.

The match kicked off around 3 p.m. on 24 May 1998 at Wembley Stadium in front of a crowd of 62,988. After twenty minutes, Grimsby took the lead through Donovan: he received a pass from Wayne Burnett, dribbled round the Northampton goalkeeper Andy Woodman, and struck the ball into the goal. Three minutes later, Jack Lester's shot went wide of the Northampton goal. Freestone's missed chance was Northampton's best opportunity to score of the half. According to Trevor Haylett, writing in The Guardian, Donovan's goal was "the only highlight of a dreadfully dull first half." Northampton started the second half the stronger of the teams missing two chances in as many minutes. Soon after the hour mark, Donovan misjudged a backpass and Freestone took possession of the ball before rounding Aidan Davison, the Grimsby goalkeeper, but missed from a tight angle. In the 78th minute, Lester was fouled in the Northampton penalty area to win a penalty. Donovan's spot kick was saved by Woodman to keep the game at 1–0. Five minute later, Paul Groves hit the Northampton crossbar but with no change the score, the match ended 1–0 and Grimsby were promoted to the First Division after a single season's absence.

Barry Stanton, an English-born rock and roll musician, immigrated to Sydney, Australia, with his family at the age of five. Known for his dynamic singing and guitar-playing, Stanton recorded material in 1957 that remained unreleased until 1988, when it was included on the compilation album *A Tribute to the King Rare Songs 1957-1965*, issued by Canetoad Records. Ainslie Baker of *The Australian Women’s Weekly* once described Stanton as having "blue eyes" and standing at 6 feet tall, with a passion for music that defined his early career. Despite his talent, Stanton's work was often overlooked compared to contemporaries like Lonnie Lee and Johnny Chester, though his singles showcased his engaging style.

Bachenbülach has a total area of 16.1 km². Of this, 33.2% is used for agricultural purposes, 39.5% is forested, 26.9% is settled with buildings or roads, and 0.4% is non-productive, including rivers, glaciers, or mountains.

He was born on January 11, 1843 at the corner of Greenwich and Carlyle Streets, New York City the son of Edward Satterlee and Jane Anna Yates, the daughter Henry Christopher Yates, an attorney-at-law; and for a number of years a New York State Senator and member of the Council of Appointment and Catharine, daughter of Johannes Mynderse and a grand niece of Joseph Christopher Yates, who was an American lawyer, politician. statesman, and founding trustee of Union College. He was also a descendant of Jellis Douwese Fonda, who emigrated in 1642 to the Dutch colony of New Netherland (New York).

The Andhra Pradesh government initiated a door-to-door survey on March 10, 2020, to identify individuals with recent travel history to COVID-19-affected countries. Conducted by village and ward volunteers, the survey lasted over a month, identifying over 32,000 individuals with influenza-like symptoms by April 19, aiming to curb the virus's spread through early detection.

On 4 January 2019, the Directorate of Galapagos National Park (DPNG) and Island Conservation reintroduced 1,436 Galápagos land iguanas (*Conolophus subcristatus*) to Santiago Island, marking the species' return after nearly 200 years of absence. The iguanas, last recorded on Santiago by Charles Darwin in 1835, had been wiped out by invasive species. Sourced from North Seymour Island, where they were introduced in the 1930s and had since overpopulated, the reintroduction aimed to restore Santiago’s ecological health and provide the iguanas with a sustainable habitat. This effort also addressed vegetation loss on North Seymour caused by the iguanas' overabundance.

Pricing for the North American version of Global Agenda was announced on October 20, 2009. The pricing model was said to reflect the games' positioning as a hybrid between a multiplayer, hub-based shooter and a larger-scale persistent world online shooter MMO. Pricing was initially planned to include an optional "Global Agenda: Conquest" subscription package.

Sergio De Randamie played high school basketball at Midland College in the United States before joining the Houston Cougars from 2004 to 2006. He began his professional career in the Netherlands with ABC Amsterdam, where he played for five years, initially receiving limited playing time. In 2011, he joined GasTerra Flames in Groningen alongside coach Hakim Salem, staying with the team for two seasons. On July 5, 2013, De Randamie signed with Landstede Basketbal in Zwolle, continuing his career in the Dutch Basketball League.
Hearst is a Nashville, Tennessee, native and graduated from MLK Magnet High School. Her mother and father both hail from the Mississippi Delta. She is married to Michael Trent.

Clement worked as a clerk in the business office of Clement and Sons Marble, the family business and a prominent company in Rutland County. He became a partner in 1871. He was also president of the Clement National Bank and the Rutland Board of Trade, and an owner of numerous railroad interests.

Beverly Foit-Albert was born on April 28, 1938, in Buffalo, New York. She received a Bachelor of Architecture from Cornell University, after which she married, had three children, then earned a Master of Architecture from the State University of New York, Buffalo. She then received a Doctor of Philosophy in Human Science, Saybrook Institute.

The cover artwork is from a 1928 anti-fascist poster designed by visual artist John Heartfield for the Communist Party of Germany. The text on the original poster is: "A hand has 5 fingers! With these 5 grab the enemy!" This slogan inspired part of the text contained on the back of the album: "The hand has five fingers, capable and powerful, with the ability to destroy as well as create". Later, it is written in bold letters: "Open your eyes, open your mouths, close your hands and make a fist" (used later by Serj Tankian in the song "Uneducated Democracy").

There are five bus routes that intersect the parish, all focusing on the town of Kington and operated by Kington-based Sargeants Brothers. Two are the south-east to north-west routes from Hereford to Llandrindod Wells, which run along the A44 Stanner Road. A circular route runs from Kington through the north-east of Kington Rural, to Combe Moor in Byton, then north-west to Presteigne, then west to New Radnor via Walton, and then south-east to Kington via the A44 in Kington Rural. A fourth route begins at Kington, and runs north-east through Kington Rural to Titley, then north into Wales, to Presteigne, Knighton, Knucklas and Lloyney. A fifth route is circular within Kington town, with a Kington Rural south-west arm running along Hergest Road to the business park in Lower Hergest.  The closest England rail connections are on the on the Welsh Marches line, at Leominster railway station 14 miles (23 km) to the west, and Hereford railway station 17 miles (27 km) to the south-east. At 10 miles (16 km) north in Wales is Knighton railway station, at Knighton, on the Heart of Wales line.

Ibex Peak, located in British Columbia, experiences a climate classified as alpine, within a temperate climate zone. Most weather fronts affecting the area originate in the Pacific Ocean and travel toward the Cascade Range, where orographic lifting causes heavy precipitation as the air rises along the windward slopes. During autumn through spring, the Cascades receive significant snowfall, with snow often persisting at higher elevations until June or July. Temperatures at Ibex Peak can occasionally drop below freezing, even in midsummer. The most favorable weather for climbing occurs from mid-June to late September, when trails are generally clear of snow and conditions are more stable.

On 7 July 1940 Krossfonn reached Lorient in German-occupied France. She was renamed Spichern and converted into a naval oiler. On 26 May 1941 during Operation Rheinübung she bunkered the German cruiser Prinz Eugen at sea with 2,660 tons of heavy fuel oil.

Dorothy Paget, born into a prominent family as the daughter of Lord Queenborough and Pauline Payne Whitney, whose lineage included the wealthy American Whitney family, was one of the most successful and eccentric figures in horse racing history. She owned Ballymacoll Stud, a renowned breeding farm located in County Meath, Ireland, and her horses won an extraordinary total of 1,532 races. Paget was the British flat racing Champion Owner in 1943 and the leading National Hunt owner in 1933-34, 1940-41, and 1951-52. She owned seven Cheltenham Gold Cup winners, including the legendary Golden Miller, who won the race five consecutive times from 1932 to 1936. Roman Hackle (1940) and Mont Tremblant (1952) also secured Gold Cup victories for her. Paget’s four Champion Hurdle winners were Insurance (1932, 1933), Solford (1940), and Distel (1946). Golden Miller also provided her with her sole Grand National victory in 1934, the same year he achieved the unprecedented feat of winning both the Cheltenham Gold Cup and the Grand National.

Todd Scott, a part-time announcer with More FM during the 1990s, was named Best New Broadcaster at the 2003 NZ Radio Awards. In 2004, as a Promotions Advisor for RadioWorks, he awarded The Edge 97.5 Wellington/WelTec Scholarship to Wellington Institute of Technology students.

Arundo donax, a fast-growing perennial grass, is highly invasive in southwestern North American rivers. Environmental scientists and land managers are concerned about its promotion as a biofuel crop due to its aggressive spread. Introduced from the Mediterranean to California in the 1820s, it was initially used for roofing material and erosion control in drainage canals in the Los Angeles area. Through vegetative reproduction and intentional plantings, it has become naturalized in warm coastal freshwater regions across North America, displacing native vegetation and altering riparian ecosystems.

The Tohono Oʼodham faced significant pressures from American ranchers, settlers, and railroads in the 19th century, as these groups sought to expand into their traditional lands. The Oʼodham had little formal documentation of land ownership, relying instead on ancestral ties and oral traditions. John F. Trudell recorded an Oʼodham man’s declaration that their land was granted by "Usen" (God) and not by any government, rejecting the concept of land grants. In the 1880s, their land holdings were further threatened by increasing settler encroachment and the expansion of railroads. However, the Oʼodham leveraged their history of cooperation with the U.S. government, particularly during the Apache Wars, to advocate for their land rights. Despite these efforts, their territory has been drastically reduced, and today, the Tohono Oʼodham Nation represents only a fraction of their ancestral lands, much of which has been militarized or repurposed.

The theory of constructed emotion (TCE) offers an explanation for how emotions emerge as the brain actively constructs them rather than reacting innately. It formulates the brain as a predictive organ, aiming to minimize prediction error by using past experiences to interpret sensory input. The computational goal of the brain, according to TCE, is to efficiently predict and categorize sensory events by updating and refining its internal model of the world. Once prediction error is minimized, the brain categorizes sensory events and constructs an emotion concept, integrating sensory input, prior learning, and language. This process involves the dynamic interaction of large-scale brain networks, including the default mode, salience, and frontoparietal networks, which are hypothesized to generate and fine-tune the brain's internal model, enabling the construction of context-specific emotional experiences.

On September 22, the owners of Belmont Stakes winner Tiz the Law announced that he would not run in the Preakness. In his absence, the favorite was Authentic, the winner of the Kentucky Derby and Haskell Invitational. When entries were taken on September 28, Authentic drew post position 9 in a field of eleven. Key rivals were:

Mood Media announced the acquisition of DMX and its subsidiary AEI Music Network Inc. on March 19, 2012, for $86.1 million, including net debt repayment. DMX, a US provider of multi-sensory branding services, creates branded connections using music, visuals, and scent, serving over 100,000 locations, including large-scale clients like AT&T and TIGO. In 2014, Stingray Digital Group acquired the Canadian assets of DMX Music Inc. from Mood Media for $16 million. These assets included music streaming services and technologies, which Stingray integrated into its global operations, significantly expanding its reach and subscriber base.

The Helmand River is the longest in Afghanistan with a length of 1,150 km. The river originates in the Hindu Kush and ends in Hamun-i-Helmand in the Sistan and Baluchistan province of neighboring Iran. One of the two primary arms of the river crosses through Lashkargah, giving it the attractive air of a riverside city. It makes for a pleasant setting for the citizens of Lashkargah to picnic. The river is deep enough at Lashkargah to allow for varied water sports, including swimming and boating. Boats are available for rent to the public. Mirwais Neka Park was recently built on the banks of the river. There is a large thicket located on the opposite side of the river from the city. Many types of trees and different species of birds, mammals, and reptiles inhabit the thicket.

Brad Davis was diagnosed with HIV in 1985 and kept his condition private to continue working and supporting his family. He died at the age of 41 on September 8, 1991, in Los Angeles. In a book proposal, Davis revealed the need to conceal his diagnosis due to industry stigma. He is interred at Forest Lawn Memorial Park in Hollywood Hills. In 1997, his wife, Susan Bluestein, disclosed that Davis had died by assisted suicide through a drug overdose.

Mitra was probably born in 1850. Little is known about his early life, except that it was carefree and open, like that of many rich people in erstwhile Bengal. According to his own testimony, Mitra was initially indifferent towards religion.

In 2007, Ferentz' Hawkeyes started 2–4 and lost their first three conference games. Highly-ranked quarterback Jake Christensen struggled replacing the graduated Tate. An upset victory over #18 Illinois ended a nine-game conference losing streak for Iowa, and the Hawkeyes closed out the Big Ten season by winning their last three conference games. However, a disappointing loss in the season finale to Western Michigan dropped the Hawkeyes' season record to 6–6. Though Iowa was bowl-eligible, the Hawkeyes did not receive a bowl bid, snapping Iowa's streak of six consecutive bowl appearances. During the summer, several key Hawkeye players were arrested and dismissed from the program for theft and misdemeanor drug charges. A high-profile sexual case involving two Hawkeye players, accused by a female swimmer, began in November.

The atoll fruit dove is the world's only dove in the tropical Pacific that has adapted exclusively to low coral atolls. It lives in forests and abandoned coconut plantations. It mainly feeds on insects and seeds, usually on the ground. This species also eats the leaves of the "tafano" or "kahaia" (Guettarda speciosa ) trees with odorous flowers.

Zack Kassian began the 2012–13 season on the Vancouver Canucks' top line alongside Henrik and Daniel Sedin, scoring five goals in his first seven games, surpassing his four-goal rookie total. However, partway through the team's second game of February, Alex Burrows replaced Kassian on the top line. Kassian was subsequently moved to the third line. The Sedin twins combined for 14 points in the first 10 games, their slowest start in nearly a decade. These changes coincided with Ryan Kesler and David Booth nearing returns from injury. The Canucks were on a six-game winning streak between late January and February 12, with Kesler expected to rejoin the lineup after their final win in that stretch.

Andros Townsend joined the Tottenham Hotspur academy at the age of eight. In January 2009, he went on loan to Yeovil Town, making his Football League debut against Milton Keynes Dons alongside fellow Spurs academy player Jonathan Obika. Townsend made 10 appearances, scoring once.

Butowsky founded the private wealth management firm Chapwood Investments in 2005. He is currently a managing partner in Chapwood Investments and created the Chapwood Index. By 2013, he was managing investments for a hundred professional athletes. Butowsky started the celebrity and athlete investor group Clubhouse Investment Club with professional baseball player Torii Hunter. Butowsky was also a managing director at Bear Stearns.

Barrie Jones, born on 10 October 1941 in Swansea, Wales, began his career with Swansea Town, signing professionally in September 1959. A right winger, he helped Swansea win the Welsh Cup in 1961. In September 1964, he transferred to Plymouth Argyle for a then-record fee of £45,000, where he played a key role in their run to the League Cup semi-finals during the 1964–65 season. Jones later joined Cardiff City in February 1967 for a transfer fee of £25,000, continuing his career as a prominent figure in Welsh football.

The 2020 Cannabis for Private Purposes Bill, introduced to Parliament on 1 September 2020, proposes limits on the personal cultivation, possession, and private use of cannabis by adults. It allows for the sharing of cannabis without remuneration and sets provisions for publicly possessing cannabis plants. The Bill also regulates gifting cannabis plants, seeds, seedlings, and dried flowers, while defining specific quantities that constitute trafficable and commercial offences, with associated penalties. Signed into law as Act 7 of 2024, it addresses constitutional concerns over prior legislation.

On the morning of September 18, 1793, at 10:00 a.m., President George Washington and his entourage crossed the Potomac River into the city of Washington, accompanied by the Alexandria Volunteer Artillery. They were joined by Masonic lodges from Virginia, Maryland, Georgetown, and Federal City, forming a grand procession with music, drums, and flying colors as they marched approximately 1.5 miles to the Capitol construction site. Upon arrival, Washington was received by Joseph Clark, the Grand Master of the Grand Lodge of Maryland. At the site, a silver plaque, crafted by Georgetown silversmith Caleb Bentley, was handed to Washington. The plaque bore an inscription dedicating the building in the "first year, of the second term, of the presidency of George Washington and in the year of Masonry 5,793," while also paying tribute to Washington's "military valor and prudence." Washington placed the plaque in the foundation trench, and the cornerstone was ceremonially lowered onto it.

The shell is very elongate-ovate, deeply umbilicated, light yellow. Its length measures 5.5 mm. The whorls of the protoconch are very deeply immersed. The five whorls of the teleoconch are very slightly rounded in the middle between the sutures, more strongly so near the anterior end and toward the summit. The summit is strongly narrowly tabulate. The periphery of the body whorl is inflated. The base of the shell is very strongly suddenly rounded, widely and deeply umbilicated. The entire surface is marked by numerous fine, closely spaced, spiral striations. The aperture is broadly ovate. The posterior angle is obtuse. The outer lip is thin. The columella is very slender, strongly curved, revolute, and provided with a deep fold a little below its insertion. The parietal wall is glazed with a thin callus.

Angelo Sala's chemistry lectures at the University of Rostock were interrupted by debates over his ideas, notably discussed by Peter Lauremberg, a professor there. Lauremberg's polite critique prompted a polemical response from an anonymous writer. Among Sala's students was Johann Lauremberg, Peter's brother, who influenced mnemotechny studies at Rostock.

Available in several different flavours, SuperGamer claims to be the "world’s first Dual Layer Live DVD". The most recent version, SuperGamer Supreme 2.5, can be downloaded as a 7.9 gigabyte iso file, while some older versions can also be downloaded in smaller sizes. As well as offering a wide selection of Linux native free games and shareware versions of commercial games, it also includes a wide selection of video card drivers, including proprietary binary blob drivers from NVIDIA and ATI as well as a wide array of free software alternatives.

The women's game also underwent a period of transition in 2008. Justine Henin, the incumbent world number one and reigning Olympic champion, retired on May 14, citing a lack of motivation for the game. Since then, three women had held the number one spot, and two, Maria Sharapova and Ana Ivanovic, had grappled with injury and poor form after successful first halves of the year. The pair were subsequently forced to withdraw from the Olympics. The favorites included Jelena Janković, who assumed the world number one ranking on August 11, the second day of the tennis tournament; Russians Svetlana Kuznetsova, the world number three, Elena Dementieva, who was a silver medalist at the 2000 Summer Olympics, and Dinara Safina, the French Open finalist who had won back-to-back tournaments in the weeks before the Olympics; and the Williams sisters Venus and Serena, who had strong Olympic pedigree having won the singles (Venus) and doubles in 2000, and who had recently played each other in the Wimbledon final.

The Critérium du Dauphiné was created in 1947 by the French newspaper *Le Dauphiné Libéré*, based in southeastern France, to boost its circulation after World War II. Designed as a preparatory race for the Tour de France, it quickly gained prominence in the cycling calendar. The inaugural edition was won by French cyclist Edouard Klabinski, setting the stage for the race's enduring legacy in professional cycling.

Peter Donat, born Pierre Collingwood Donat on January 20, 1928, in Kentville, Nova Scotia, was the son of Philip Ernst Donat, a landscape gardener, and his wife. His younger brother, Richard Donat, is also an actor, known for his role in the television series *Haven*. Peter’s uncle, Robert Donat, was a British actor who won an Oscar in 1939 for *Goodbye, Mr. Chips*. Peter emigrated to the United States in 1950 and studied drama at Yale University. He first gained attention as a stage actor in *Cyrano de Bergerac* and, in 1961, played a leading role in Donald Jack's *The Canvas Barricade*, the first Canadian play performed at the Stratford Festival.

As of the 2000 census, Randolph County, Alabama, had a population of 22,380, with 8,653 households and 6,319 families residing in the county. The population density was 42 people per square mile, with 10,940 housing units at an average density of 20 per square mile. The racial makeup was 74.96% White (non-Hispanic), 23.66% Black or African American, 0.29% Native American, 0.16% Asian, 0.38% from other races, and 0.55% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race made up 1.02% of the population.

Emlen Tunnell was born in Bryn Mawr, Pennsylvania, though conflicting records make his exact birth year uncertain. His tombstone, the Social Security Death Index, and the Department of Veterans Affairs BIRLS Death File list his birth date as March 29, 1924. However, official NFL records and multiple sources in the early 1960s reported it as March 29, 1922, while Tunnell himself listed March 29, 1923, on both his 1942 draft card and his 1950 application for World War II benefits. Other sources suggest 1925 as his birth year. In 1961, Tunnell joked about his age, saying, "I'm not really 41, as everyone says. I'm closer to 42," further adding to the uncertainty surrounding his birth year.

On the 19th, an F-4G fired an AGM-88 HARM at an Iraqi SAM site after a 14 nm lock-on east of Mosul. An hour later, an F-16C was fired upon by AAA but not hit. Two hours later a section of F-16C's were fired upon and dropped cluster bombs on guns north of Mosul. Iraq then later called a cease fire to celebrate Clinton's inauguration which took place on the 20th of January.

Scott Dobie, born in Workington, joined Carlisle United as an apprentice in June 1995 and turned professional in 1997. While at Carlisle, his goalbound header in the final game of the 1998–99 season led to Jimmy Glass scoring a dramatic last-minute goal, securing a 2-1 win over Plymouth Argyle and saving the club from relegation. During the 1998–99 season, Dobie attracted interest from clubs like Preston and Barnsley, with manager Ian Atkins valuing him at £2 million and emphasizing his potential for further growth.

The 18th edition of the Prêmio Angelo Agostini took place in 2002 at the Comix Book Shop. Traditionally awarding three 'Masters of National Comics,' this edition honored 13 individuals to celebrate the award's 18th anniversary, setting a record for the most recipients in this category in a single year.

Since its discovery in August 1950 by R/V John N. Cobb, Cobb Seamount has been the target of passing cruises and sampling missions, totaling to over 927 km (576 mi) of soundings and dozens of samples by 1967. In 1968, Project Sea Use, a multi-party expedition aboard the United States Coast and Geodetic Survey oceanographic research vessel USC&GS Oceanographer (R 101), visited the seamount, and much of what was initially known about it stems from the expedition. This was followed in 1970, two expeditions in the late 1970s, and 1992, however all have been fairly limited in scope. In 2012, an extensive scientific survey of Cobb Seamount was led jointly by Fisheries and Oceans Canada (DFO) and the United States National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). The survey used, among other methods, remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) and autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) to conduct high-resolution surveys of the benthic (seafloor) community above a depth of 1200 meters (3,937 feet).

The airport was dedicated on 26 October 1945. Airline flights began in 1954. The terminal building built in 1960 was used until the present terminal was completed in 1987. By 1969, Willard was the second-busiest airport in the state of Illinois. After the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978, many airlines found service to small airports to be inefficient, the price differential to airfares from major hub airports such as Chicago O'Hare grew, which limited demand for tickets from Willard and caused airlines to leave.

The runic inscription U 1050, listed in the Rundata catalog, is carved on a granite stone standing 1.8 meters tall. The inscription, in runestone style Pr 4 (Urnes style), features text within a serpent motif. It is unsigned but attributed to the runemaster Fot, who also carved U 1049 at the same church and U 778 in Tibble. The style and craftsmanship link these inscriptions to the late Viking Age.

Hirsi grew up in Minneapolis, Minnesota, and is the daughter of Somali American U.S. Congresswoman Ilhan Omar and Ahmed Abdisalan Hirsi. At the age of 12, she was one of the participants protesting for justice for Jamar Clark at the Mall of America. Hirsi attended Minneapolis South High School, from which she graduated in 2021. She became involved in climate activism after joining her high school's environmental club in her freshman year.

The Debut is based on a ten-minute short film Cajayon had made as his thesis project at Loyola Marymount University in Los Angeles, California, United States. He incorporated themes from co-writer John Manal Castro's short film, Diary of a Gangsta Sucka. The full-length film took eight years to produce and raise funding for (from 1992 to 2000), another year to be released in theaters, and two years to go on DVD in 2003 and television.

Fiserv is headquartered in Brookfield. The firm provides financial services technology (software, for the most part) for banks, thrifts, credit unions, securities broker dealers, leasing and finance companies, and retailers, among others. Its 2016 revenue was approximately $5.5 billion.

Hoplites carried a short sword, typically a xiphos, as a secondary weapon to their primary spear, the dory. The xiphos, with its dual-edged, leaf-shaped blade, was designed for close-quarters combat when the spear was broken, lost, or rendered ineffective in tight formations. While the standard xiphos blade measured around 60 centimeters (24 inches), the Spartan version was notably shorter, ranging from 30 to 45 centimeters (12 to 18 inches). This reduced length made it highly maneuverable and effective in the dense press of battle, allowing warriors to thrust through gaps in the shield wall to target vulnerable areas like the groin or throat. Such a compact weapon became particularly advantageous during the Peloponnesian War, as many hoplites began to abandon heavy body armor, increasing the importance of precise strikes. Alternatively, some hoplites carried a kopis, a heavier, curved sword suited for cutting. The xiphos was typically sheathed in a scabbard, known as a baldric, worn under the left arm for easy access.

This owl feeds mainly on large insects, and they are thought to feed on small vertebrates as well. Bats are estimated to represent only 5% of owl's diet, but they are known to be opportunistic predators. Recent observations described two crested owl preying on Seba's short tailed bat in mist nets.

Former Guns N' Roses guitarist Slash performed "Bent To Fly" from World on Fire, with the accompanying vocal track by Myles Kennedy being played over loudspeaker. The performance of the song was accompanied by a montage of the 2014 NRL season on ANZ Stadium's screens. Slash also performed the guitar riff from Guns N' Roses' "Sweet Child o' Mine", before closing the set with an improvised solo.

In February 2012, LL Social, an online marketing blog, broke the news that Pinterest was using Skimlinks to monetize content posted by its users. Commercial links included in Pins were being affiliated via Skimlinks. Although the partnership had been ongoing for 2 years, Pinterest had just recently grown exponentially in popularity and the news was badly received. Most of the backlash centered on the company's failure to disclose its monetization practices rather than its decision to monetize users' content. Though it was claimed that the revelations prompted Pinterest to drop its affiliation with Skimlinks, Pinterest's CEO, Ben Silbermann, clarified that they had actually been using Skimlinks as a test while they explored various monetization solutions and had stopped using the service a week before the news broke.

In 1770 Strachey married Jane, only daughter of Capt. John Kelsall (1702-1787), the widow of Capt. Thomas Latham. They had three sons and one daughter. His second son Edward Strachey was the father of John Strachey and Lieutenant-General Sir Richard Strachey and the grandfather of Lytton Strachey, James Strachey, Oliver Strachey and Dorothy Bussy. Other descendants of Strachey include the Liberal politician Edward Strachey, 1st Baron Strachie, the journalist John Strachey and the Labour politician John Strachey. Lady Strachey died on 12 February 1824.

MIDV was first isolated from the mosquito species Ochlerotatus (Aedes) caballus, since then it has been found in other mosquito species in South Africa including: Aedes leneatopennis and Aedes albothorax. The virus is limited to Africa, but due to the wide range of these mosquito hosts and possible travelling of horses and livestock, there is cause to believe it might spread elsewhere. Sindbis virus, an extremely similar virus to MIDV has spread outside of Africa and effects humans. The horses themselves cannot act as vectors to spread MIDV since the concentration of virus in the host blood stream is too small to infect a mosquito.

There is one exclave, the tiny village of Barak (population 627), in the Fergana Valley. The village is surrounded by Uzbek territory. It is located on the road from Osh (Kyrgyzstan) to Khodjaabad (Uzbekistan) about 4 kilometres (2 miles) north-west from the Kyrgyz–Uzbek border in the direction of Andijan. Barak is administratively part of Kara-Suu District in Kyrgyzstan's Osh Region.

He was born in Beulah, near Newcastle Emlyn, Cardiganshire, the only son of David Jones and Margaret Thomas. He attended the local village school in Trewen before going to Pencader Grammar School in 1893. In 1896 he went up to University College, Aberystwyth, to study physics, graduating in 1900. He then went to Trinity College, Cambridge, and was awarded a B.A. degree in Natural Sciences (geology) in 1902.

Trastuzumab deruxtecan received FDA approval on December 20, 2019, based on the DESTINY-Breast01 trial, which enrolled 184 female participants with HER2-positive, unresectable, or metastatic breast cancer. Participants had received two or more prior anti-HER2 therapies in the metastatic setting. Administered every three weeks at 5.4 mg/kg, trastuzumab deruxtecan achieved an overall response rate of 61%, with a median duration of response of 14.8 months. Tumor imaging was conducted every six weeks. The trial demonstrated significant tumor reduction, leading to accelerated approval under the condition of further studies to confirm its clinical benefit.

Bibiana Campos Seijo began her publishing career in the UK as a technical editor at the European Respiratory Society. Her first Editor-in-Chief role was at *Chemistry World*, published by the Royal Society of Chemistry, a position she assumed in 2013. In 2014, she became Editor-in-Chief of *Chemical & Engineering News* (C&EN), published by the American Chemical Society, and relocated to Washington, DC, to take on this role.

Jennifer's career as a Billboard 100 pop singer began with her first single, "Crush". The song, written by Andy Goldmark, Mark Mueller and Cosgrove & Clark was initially played by KIIS-FM in Los Angeles in April, 1998 and went on to be the most-requested song on the radio station that summer. It ultimately became a Top 3 Billboard chart hit in the U.S., logged 17 weeks in Billboard magazine's Top 10 and went to #1 in Australia, Canada and New Zealand.

The Perth Heat were the first team to clinch a postseason position when they defeated the Brisbane Bandits in Perth on 8 January, and then clinched a first place finish in their next game when they defeated the Canberra Cavalry in Canberra on 12 January. The three remaining positions in the postseason, and the teams that would take them, were not determined until the last game was finished. The Melbourne Aces finished a half-game ahead of a four-way tie between the Adelaide Bite, Brisbane Bandits, Canberra Cavalry and Sydney Blue Sox to secure the remaining spot in the major semi-final. The Bite and the Blue Sox finished third and fourth respectively after the ABL's tiebreakers were applied.

The 2007 season marked a challenging start for Kirk Ferentz's Hawkeyes, who opened with a 2-4 record. Quarterback Jake Christensen struggled to fill the void left by the graduated Drew Tate, leading to inconsistent offensive performances. However, the Hawkeyes ended a nine-game Big Ten losing streak with a surprising upset over Illinois. Despite this, Iowa finished 4-4 in conference play and suffered a shocking season-finale loss to Western Michigan, leaving them with a 6-6 overall record. Although bowl-eligible, the Hawkeyes did not receive a bowl bid, marking a disappointing end to the season. Compounding the difficulties, the summer of 2007 was marred by legal troubles for several key players, including arrests and suspensions, which cast a shadow over the program's efforts to rebuild.

Cobb Seamount, an ancient volcano in the northeast Pacific Ocean, was discovered in 1950 by the vessel *M.V. John N. Cobb*. By 1967, investigations of the seamount had amassed over 927 kilometers of soundings, 34 bottom samples, 25 hydrographic stations, underwater photography, and two current time studies. In 1968, the seamount was visited by the vessel *T.G. Thompson* as part of a scientific expedition. Subsequent expeditions to Cobb Seamount occurred in 1973 and 1990, with notable activities including the placement of acoustic sources for oceanographic experiments. In 2012, Fisheries and Oceans Canada, in collaboration with NOAA Fisheries' Northwest and Southwest Fisheries Science Centers, conducted an extensive survey of Cobb Seamount. This expedition utilized a remotely operated vehicle (ROV) and an autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) to perform high-resolution surveys of the benthic community, revealing a habitat rich in deep-sea coral.

Each team plays 162 games per season. A team's schedule is typically organized into three-game series, sets of consecutive games against the same opponent, with occasional two- or four-game series. Postponed games or continuations of suspended games can result in an ad hoc one-game or five-game series. All games of a series are usually hosted by the same team and multiple series are typically grouped together. I.e, a team usually hosts several series in a row, called a homestand, and follows that by going on several road series in a row. Teams generally play games five to seven days per week. Most games are scheduled at night, although teams will often play day games on Opening Day, holidays, and for the last game of a series to allow teams extra time to travel to their next opponent. Sunday games are generally played during the afternoon, allowing teams to travel to their next destination prior to a Monday night game.

At the 1976 World Championships in Athletics in Malmö, Veniamin Soldatenko of the Soviet Union won gold, becoming the oldest male athlete to claim an IAAF world champion title at age 37. Mexico’s Enrique Vera took silver, while Reima Salonen of Finland earned bronze. Of the 42 walkers who entered the 50km race, 33 finished, 6 failed to complete, and 3 were disqualified.

Sir Henry Strachey, 1st Baronet, married Jane (née Kelsall) Latham, the daughter of John Kelsall, in 1770. Jane was previously married to Captain Thomas Latham. Together, Henry and Jane had five children: three sons—Henry (1772–1858), Edward (1774–1832), and Richard (1781–1847)—and two daughters, Charlotte-Margaret (ca. 1771–1801) and Lucy (1782–1863). Edward Strachey’s children included Sir Richard Strachey, and his grandchildren encompassed figures such as Sir Edward Strachey, 3rd Baronet. Lady Strachey passed away on 24 February 1824.

The capital structure of a company refers to the mix of debt and equity used to finance its operations, reflecting how much is funded by creditors versus owners. While incorporating some level of debt can enhance shareholder returns and support business growth by providing access to additional capital, excessive debt poses significant risks, such as reduced flexibility, higher default likelihood, and constraints on value-creating investments. For companies with international operations, capital structure decisions become more complex due to varying tax regimes, economic conditions, and financial systems across borders. Raj Aggarwal has demonstrated that the average levels of corporate debt differ significantly by region, influenced by factors such as national preferences for bank- versus market-oriented financing systems, tax policies, and economic stability. His research on the 300 largest banks globally revealed that their financing activities are primarily determined by two factors: the regulatory environment and the economic conditions of their home countries. These findings highlight the importance of tailoring capital structure strategies to regional and industry-specific contexts, as well as the need for companies to balance the benefits of debt with the risks it introduces, particularly in a globalized financial landscape.

Middelburg virus (MIDV) was first isolated from *Aedes caballus* mosquitoes in South Africa, where other species such as *Ae. circumluteolus* and *Ma. africana* have also been found to carry the virus. Currently limited to Africa, MIDV's potential spread outside the continent is concerning, as its close relative, Sindbis virus (SINV), has already expanded beyond Africa. SINV causes mild symptoms in humans, including fever, rash, and arthralgia. Horses, while susceptible to MIDV and capable of severe disease, cannot act as vectors due to their inability to transmit the virus. Migratory birds and ornithophilic mosquitoes are key amplifying hosts for MIDV.

The club shares Lynn Family Stadium and its training facilities with Louisville City FC. The soccer-specific stadium, opened in 2020, has a seated capacity of 11,600, with standing-room capacity up to 15,304. The 20-acre (8.1 ha) training facilities at Louisville Champions Park includes several practice fields and an indoor gymnasium and office complex.

Robert N. Bellah was elected a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences in 1967 and to the American Philosophical Society in 1996. In 2000, President Bill Clinton awarded him the National Humanities Medal for his contributions to understanding community and social responsibility in American society. In 2007, he received the Martin E. Marty Award for the Public Understanding of Religion from the American Academy of Religion. In 2008, he was awarded an honorary doctorate by the Max-Weber-Kolleg in Erfurt, Germany.

On December 13, 1915, Herman defeated fellow New Orleans boxer Eddie Coulon in a fourth-round knockout in New Orleans. Both boxers weighed 116 pounds, but Coulon had a 3+1⁄2-inch reach advantage. Herman had faced Coulon twice in his early career on September 30, and October 21, 1912, in two six-round draws in New Orleans. He would defeat to Coulon on September 1, 1913, in Memphis, Tennessee, in an eight-round points decision.

At the medieval University of Oxford, students organized themselves into two nations: the Australes and the Boreales. The Australes, representing southern England, included scholars from the Romance lands and the Celtic Fringe, such as the Welsh. The Boreales primarily came from northern England. The Australes were the more powerful group, reflecting the dominance of southern England during the period.

Hope Larson, of German and Swedish descent, grew up in Asheville, North Carolina, and attended Carolina Day School. After high school, she enrolled at Rochester Institute of Technology before transferring to the School of the Art Institute of Chicago, where she earned a BFA in 2004. Following graduation, she moved to Toronto with her husband, Bryan Lee O'Malley, and in 2005, they relocated to Halifax, Nova Scotia.

At the 2014 NRL Grand Final, Slash performed his single "Bent To Fly" from *World on Fire*, with Myles Kennedy's vocal track played over loudspeakers as Kennedy was touring with Alter Bridge. ANZ Stadium's screens displayed NRL season highlights during the performance. Slash followed with the iconic opening riff of Guns N' Roses' "Sweet Child O' Mine," concluding his set with a solo performance accompanied by pyrotechnics.

Robert Swindells, born on March 20, 1939, in Bradford, England, left school at 15 to work for a local newspaper. He later served in the Royal Air Force and held various jobs before training as a primary school teacher. While in training, he wrote his first novel, *When Darkness Comes*, published by Brockhampton Press in 1973. Swindells combined teaching with writing until 1980, when he became a full-time author.

The events of *Ristar* take place in the Valdi System, where the evil space pirate Kaiser Greedy has used a mind-control machine to enslave the leaders of its planets. In the Japanese version, the inhabitants of Planet Neer pray to the Star Goddess, Oruto, for help. She receives their desperate prayers and awakens her child, Ristar, to save the galaxy. Greedy’s mind-control minion, Riho, kidnaps the planet elder, furthering the chaos. In contrast, the English version omits Oruto entirely, instead referencing Ristar’s father, a legendary hero, as the source of his inspiration. Despite these differences, Ristar’s mission remains the same: to defeat Greedy and his brainwashed henchmen, liberate the enslaved planets, and restore peace to the Valdi System. Ristar accomplishes this by using his extendable arms to grab enemies and obstacles, delivering powerful headbutts to overcome challenges.

The Department of Correctional Services (DCS) manages Jamaica's corrections system, contributing to national security by implementing rehabilitation and reintegration programmes in the institutions while maintaining the safe custody of offenders. This administration is led by the Commissioner of Corrections, Ina Hunter, who works closely with the Permanent Secretary, Dianne McIntosh, and Charles. Since his appointment, he has sought ways to improve the rehabilitation of offenders to prepare them for life after release.

Pan, known for his ability to incite fear, inspired panic with his angry shout when disturbed during his secluded naps. Following the Titans' assault on Olympus, he claimed credit for the gods' victory by sowing fear and disorder among their enemies. In the Battle of Marathon (490 BCE), Pan favored the Athenians, instilling panic in their Persian adversaries, aiding in their triumph.

The closing ceremony of the 2010 Asian Games began on November 27, 2010, at 8:06 PM local time at the Haixinsha Island in Guangzhou, China, with an audience of 80,000 spectators. The theme of the ceremony was "Please Leave Your Song Behind," celebrating cultural unity. Cultural displays from China, India, Indonesia, and Japan were featured, including Indian Punjabi folk songs, the Indonesian song "Bengawan Solo," and the Japanese song "Sakura." The performance of "Triumphant Return" was a highlight, featuring artists from across Asia. The event marked the end of the 16-day Games, showcasing the spirit of togetherness and artistic excellence.

David Zurawik of *The Baltimore Sun* praised *Scout’s Honor* for its "most impressive accomplishment" of exposing the inconsistency within the Boy Scouts of America, an institution that preaches values of inclusion while enforcing exclusionary policies. Zurawik noted that the film celebrates the courage and determination of young Steven Cozza and his grassroots movement, "Scouting for All," in challenging these policies. Director Tom Shepard was commended by *Film Threat* for his nuanced portrayal of Cozza, depicting him as an honest and genuine young man raised with strong values. Donald Liebenson of the *Chicago Tribune* highlighted the film’s exploration of the Boy Scouts’ controversial stance on LGBTQ+ inclusion and its broader implications for societal values. While Shepard’s storytelling was lauded for its emotional depth, some critics, including *Film Threat*, expressed a desire for a more balanced perspective, noting the limited representation of those opposing Cozza’s cause. Despite this, the film remains a powerful testament to perseverance and moral conviction, with Cozza’s transformation from a hesitant teen to a confident advocate serving as its emotional core.

The capital structure of a company is the proportion of its assets financed with other people's money, also defined as the proportion of its capitalization financed by long-term debt. Too little debt often means foregoing the tax, monitoring, and other advantages of debt, a less expensive form of capital compared to equity. However, too much debt can expose a company to a higher than acceptable risk of default or not being able to pay its creditors (who can then sue to bankrupt the company). Trade-offs like these become more complicated when companies have operations and debt in many countries. Aggarwal has been writing about this topic for many years, and has demonstrated that average levels of debt used by companies differ in various countries in Asia, Europe, and Latin America. Additionally, he has been able to show that this average proportion of corporate debt varies across national borders depending on a number of factors including the level of disclosure timeliness, institutional trading activities, and enforcement of anti-insider trading laws. Finally, Aggarwal showed that financing activities by the 300 largest banks in the world are determined first by the location (country) of the bank and second by the bank's size itself.

De Randamie played high school basketball for Midland CC and later college basketball for the Houston Cougars from 2004 until 2006. De Randamie started his professional career in the Netherlands with ABC Amsterdam in the Dutch Basketball League. De Randamie played four years for the team, first as a back-up but later as a starter. In 2011, De Randamie left for GasTerra Flames in Groningen along with his Amsterdam coach Hakim Salem. De Randamie played two years for Groningen. At June 11, 2013 he signed a contract with Landstede Basketbal from Zwolle.

Duerson owned three McDonald's restaurants in Louisville, Kentucky for six months, from late 1994 to April 1995. He purchased the majority interest in Fair Oaks Farms (formerly Brooks Sausage Company) in 1995. He sold his stake in the company in 2002 and started Duerson Foods, but that company was forced into receivership in 2006 and most of its assets were auctioned off.

Born in Workington, Cumbria, Dobie joined Carlisle United as an apprentice in June 1995, turning professional on 10 May 1997. While at Carlisle, it was Dobie's goalbound header that was parried into the path of Carlisle goalkeeper Jimmy Glass, who scored with the last kick of the final game of the 1998–99 season, preserving Carlisle's Football League status and relegating Scarborough. During 2000–01, Dobie attracted interest from other clubs, with Carlisle manager Ian Atkins claiming that the player could be worth as much as £2 million in the transfer market.

GoodTimes Entertainment, originally founded as GoodTimes Home Video Corporation in 1984 by the Cayre brothers, began with the distribution of 25 public domain video titles, capitalizing on the growing home video market. The company later expanded its offerings to include low-priced fitness videos, such as the "29 Minute Workout" series, and became best known for its low-budget traditionally animated films produced in collaboration with studios like Jetlag Productions, Golden Films, and Blye Migicovsky Productions. Many of its home-video releases, including titles like *Aladdin*, *Beauty and the Beast*, *Pinocchio*, *The Little Mermaid*, and *Thumbelina*, bore names similar to major animated films from other studios, often released around the same time. This strategy led to a 1993 lawsuit from The Walt Disney Company, which alleged that GoodTimes' VHS packaging too closely resembled Disney's, potentially confusing consumers. However, the court ruled in favor of GoodTimes, determining that the packaging similarities were insufficient to prove Disney's claims of trade dress infringement and consumer confusion.

During the Islamic Golden Age, the Abbasid Empire, under caliphs like Harun al-Rashid and al-Mamun, established the House of Wisdom in Baghdad, fostering the translation of foreign works into Arabic. Christian scholar Hunayn ibn Ishaq (808–873) led this effort, translating 116 texts, including works by Plato, Aristotle, Galen, and Hippocrates. His contributions, alongside a vast library network, made Greek philosophy and science accessible to the Islamic world.

On June 4, 1991, Philip Rastelli was granted a compassionate release from the Federal Medical Center in Springfield, Missouri. He died of liver cancer on June 24, 1991, at Booth Memorial Hospital in Flushing, Queens, at the age of 73. Rastelli is buried at Saint John Cemetery in Middle Village, Queens. Following his death, Joseph Massino succeeded him as boss of the Bonanno crime family.

Brookfield, Wisconsin, is home to Fiserv, Inc., a global leader in financial and payments services. Serving industries like banking, fintech, and retail, Fiserv reported approximately $5.5 billion in revenue in 2016, driven by innovation and technology solutions.

Gerhard Bast, son of attorney Rudolf Bast, grew up in a German national household. In 1912, the family moved to Amstetten. Bast studied at the high school in Wels. After graduation he studied jurisprudence at the University of Graz, where he was a member of the burschenschaft (student fraternity) "Germania Graz". He graduated law school in 1935 with his doctorate. In October 1931, he became a member of the NSDAP (member number 612,972) and shortly thereafter joined the SS (SS Number 23,064). After graduation, Bast worked at the county court in St. Pölten, but lost his job shortly afterwards due to his membership in the Nazi Party. Bast then worked in his father's law office, who was an enthusiastic national socialist.

In Major League Baseball (MLB), each team plays 162 games during the regular season, which spans approximately six months. A team's schedule is organized into series, typically consisting of three or four games, though variations such as two-game, one-game, or five-game series can occur due to factors like makeup games or scheduling constraints. All games in a series are usually hosted by the same team, and a homestand refers to a stretch of consecutive home games for a team. Teams generally play six to seven days per week, with most games scheduled in the evening to maximize fan attendance. However, day games are common on weekends, particularly on Sundays, when games are generally played in the afternoon. This scheduling accommodates travel for teams and ensures players have adequate rest before the next series. Sunday afternoon games typically start at 1:00 p.m. ET, with some flexibility for later starts, depending on broadcast considerations or doubleheaders.

Rouch was ordained in the Church of England as a deacon in 1999 and as a priest in 2000. He served his curacy at St John the Evangelist, East Dulwich in the Diocese of Southwark from 1999 to 2002. He was on the staff of St Stephen's House, Oxford as a junior research fellow from 2002 to 2004. During this time, he began studying for a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) degree at the University of Manchester, which he completed in 2005 with a doctoral thesis titled "The Christian doctrine of time in negotiation with contemporary physics ". He was also chaplain to St John's College, Oxford for the 2003/2004 academic year.

Pendred started his professional career in 2009. He became the number one ranked welterweight in Ireland during 2010 after winning the national title. In March 2013 he became the Cage Warriors Welterweight Champion after beating the title holder Gael Grimaud in London, England.

The Kunstmuseum Den Haag hosts 25 to 30 exhibitions annually. In 2021 and 2022, exhibitions featured Portuguese painter Paula Rego, Basque fashion designer Cristobal Balenciaga, and English artist Grayson Perry. The 2021 exhibition "Monet: The Garden Paintings" was voted the Netherlands' best museum exhibition.

In 1948, the British post-war government realised the need for an armed forces larger than that which voluntary recruitment could provide. Discussions were soon started in parliament on a new National Service Act with a first such act being approved in July 1947. This first version was to come into force on 1 January 1949 and established the period of National Service to 12 months. However, financial crises, the advent of the Cold War and the Malayan Emergency caused the act to be amended before coming into force. The amendment was approved in December 1948, with the date in which it would come into force still being 1 January 1949.

In 1861, Edmund Lander leased the area known as the Mooloolah Back Plain, now Landers Shoot, from the Queensland Government. Pioneers like Lander harvested timber along the Blackall Range, stripping the bark to expose the inner layers and shaping one end of the logs for transport. The prepared trees were pushed into timber chutes, which used gravity to efficiently move logs down the slopes to collection points. These chutes, essential for reducing friction and speeding up delivery, made Landers Shoot a critical hub in the timber harvesting process. Originally called Mooloolah Back Plain, the region played a significant role in the early timber industry of Queensland.

Born in Swansea, Jones began his career at his home town team Swansea Town, signing professional with the club in September 1959. A skilful winger, he soon established himself in the squad, winning the Welsh Cup in 1961, and was transferred to Plymouth Argyle in 1964 for £45,000; which was a club record fee for Plymouth and a British record fee for a winger at the time. He spent three years at the club, reaching the Football League Cup semi-finals during the 1964–65 season, before returning to South Wales in March 1967, joining Cardiff City for £25,000.

Saracens Women were formed in 1989 by a group of players, including five senior internationals. Starting in the national 7s competition, they won the title in their first season and reached the semi-finals of the national cup, losing to rivals Richmond. They entered the Women's Premiership in 1994 and secured their first title in 1997. In 2007, Saracens Ladies II faced challenges but achieved a significant victory over Bath Rugby Ladies, ending Bath's unbeaten run. Saracens Women have since become the most successful team in the league, winning 13 titles, with Richmond remaining one of their key rivals.

In 2011, Michelle Zauner formed the Philadelphia-based emo band Little Big League with Ian Dykstra, Kevin O'Halloran, and Deven Craige. Zauner and O'Halloran met in classes at Bryn Mawr College, while she met Craige at a show for her previous band, Post Post, and Dykstra at a party. The band released an eponymous EP on April 1, 2012, and recorded their debut studio album, *These Are Good People*, at Craige's studio in Berk's Warehouse, Philadelphia, finishing in January 2013. The album was released on August 6, 2013, through the Tiny Engines label and featured the single "My Very Own You," which was accompanied by a music video. Little Big League’s energetic and emotionally resonant sound gained attention, but Zauner left the band in 2014 to care for her ailing mother.

Seymour R. Thaler, elected to the New York Supreme Court in November 1971, was indicted on December 21, 1971, alongside three associates for selling $800,000 in stolen Treasury bills. He pleaded not guilty on December 23, 1971, and in February 1972, four counts of perjury were added to his indictment. On March 23, 1972, Thaler was convicted of unlawfully receiving and disposing of stolen securities and perjury. Sentenced on August 8, 1972, he received three years in prison. Disbarred on October 26, 1972, by the Appellate Division, his conviction was upheld by the United States Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit on March 5, 1973.

In December 2013, Cairns was the subject of allegations in an ICC investigation into match-fixing. He is alleged to have attempted to manipulate games in India when he was captain of the Chandigarh Lions in the short lived Indian Cricket League. Cairns has rejected these claims.

As of the census of 2000, there were 5,259 people, 1,788 households, and 1,534 families residing in the township. The population density was 392.5 people per square mile (151.5/km2). There were 1,821 housing units at an average density of 135.9/sq mi (52.5/km2). The racial makeup of the township was 96.98% White, 0.89% African American, 0.17% Native American, 1.20% Asian, 0.13% from other races, and 0.63% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 1.39% of the population.

Jairam Valjee carried on his father's legacy and earned a name as a railway contractor, especially working for Bengal Nagpur Railway completing several bridges and doubling of railway lines. In years 1921–29, he camped near Paraghat for construction of railway bridge over Arpa river and doubling of rail lines of Bilaspur-Raigarh section. He used to work as railway contractor with his main office at Raigarh and there is a colony named after him called Jairam Valjee Colony at Raigarh. He often worked with fellow Mistri railway contractors like Rai Saheb Mulji Jagmal Sawaria of Bilaspur and Shamji Gangji Sawaria of Raigarh.

Arundo is a highly invasive plant in southwestern North American rivers, and its promotion as a biofuel in other regions is of great concern to environmental scientists and land managers. Arundo donax was introduced from the Mediterranean to California in the 1820s for roofing material and erosion control in drainage canals in the Los Angeles area. Through spread and subsequent plantings as an ornamental plant, and for use as reeds in woodwind instruments, it has become naturalised throughout warm coastal freshwaters of North America, and its range continues to spread.

In 1996, he entered politics by becoming adviser to Prachuap Chaiyasan (the Minister of Foreign Affairs) and served as Deputy Minister of Public Health from 2004 to 2005 and Deputy Minister of Commerce in 2004. He was then banned from political office for five years due to his membership of the Thai Rak Thai Party. Anutin then joined the Bhumjaithai Party in 2012 after the expiration of the ban and was elected as the leader of the Bhumjaithai Party on 14 October 2012.

Beverly "Bonnie" Foit-Albert, born in Buffalo, New York, graduated from Cornell University with a Bachelor of Architecture in 1961. After marrying and having three children, she earned a Master of Architecture from the University at Buffalo in 1975 and later a Ph.D. in Human Sciences from California's Saybrook Institute through a UB program.

The RECLAIM Act was preceded by and amends the SMCRA, which was enacted in 1977 to decrease the amount of coal dust in downstream water resources and other mining operations that are harmful to wildlife. Shortly after SMCRA was established, the United States Department of Interior created the Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE) agency to enforce the law. OSMRE's purview includes protecting wildlife, restoring abandoned mines, and making sure the regulations for surface coal mining are complied with.

As of the 2000 census, Middleport, Pennsylvania, had a population of 458, with 202 households and 123 families residing in the borough. The population density was 849 people per square mile, with 238 housing units at an average density of 440.7 per square mile. The racial makeup was 98.69% White, 0.22% Native American, and 1.09% from other races, with 1.53% of the population identifying as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

During the 2013 offseason, LaMarcus Aldridge expressed his desire to remain with the Portland Trail Blazers despite being the subject of trade rumors. However, he also candidly requested improvements to the team’s roster to enhance their competitiveness. Aldridge began his eighth NBA season with strong performances, recording five consecutive double-doubles from November 9 to 17. On November 23, 2013, during a game against the Golden State Warriors, Aldridge was involved in an altercation after Warriors center Andrew Bogut became entangled with Joel Freeland. As a result, Aldridge was fined $45,000 for his role in the incident. Two days later, on November 25, 2013, he was named Western Conference Player of the Week for the sixth time in his career. During the Blazers’ 11-game win streak that season, Aldridge averaged 22.8 points, 9.8 rebounds, and 2.5 assists per game. On December 12, 2013, he achieved a historic milestone by scoring 31 points and grabbing a career-high 25 rebounds in a victory over the Houston Rockets, becoming the first player in franchise history to record at least 30 points and 25 rebounds in a game. Aldridge continued his stellar play, recording a career-high 44 points along with 13 rebounds, 5 assists, and 2 blocks on January 23, 2014, in a win against the Denver Nuggets.

Židovska meditacija – istraživanje mističnih staza judaizma was published in 2003. The book provides an overview of different meditation techniques to the interpretation of many concepts of Jewish mysticism. The book has a kabbalistic vocabulary.

White Wolf, originally formed as Warrior in 1975, later changed their name to White Wolf before signing with RCA Records. Their debut album, *Standing Alone*, was released in 1984, featuring the minor hit "Shadows in the Night." The band toured extensively in support of the album, performing in both the United States and Canada.

Actor Gerard McCarthy initially feared backlash in his native Belfast over Kris Fisher's flamboyant, bisexual, cross-dressing persona but was pleasantly surprised by praise, even from "macho men." McCarthy noted that fans appreciated Kris's outspoken nature, with some expressing admiration for his boldness. The *Radio Times* highlighted Kris's involvement in a love triangle, describing his behavior as embodying "free love" but also reinforcing stereotypes about bisexual promiscuity. Kris shared a clinch with Ravi before falling into Nancy's arms, showcasing his complex romantic entanglements. Despite initial concerns, Kris's unapologetic individuality resonated with audiences, solidifying his status as a beloved and controversial character.

In 1946, the British post-war Labour government recognized the need for a larger armed forces to address peacetime demands, including post-war occupation duties and global commitments. Discussions in Parliament began, leading to the approval of the National Service Act in July 1947. The Act, initially set to come into force in January 1949, required one year of service in the armed forces followed by five years in the reserve. However, financial crises, the Cold War, and the Malaya Emergency prompted the National Service Amendment Act in December 1948, extending the service period to 18 months to enhance efficiency and support overseas operations.

Rocks from the Paleogene and Neogene periods are absent in Wisconsin, but the state’s geology was profoundly shaped by the Wisconsin Glaciation, which began about 100,000 years ago and ended around 11,000 years ago. During this time, glacial lobes, including the Green Bay, Lake Michigan, Des Moines, and Superior lobes, carved out features like Green Bay, the Lake Winnebago Basin, and Horicon Marsh. These lobes deposited sediments up to hundreds of feet deep. However, the Driftless Area in central and southwestern Wisconsin remained unglaciated, preserving its unique karst topography. The glaciation left behind silt, gravel, and boulders, forming the landscape seen today, with the ice fully retreating from northern Wisconsin approximately 10,000 years ago.

Jameson first received public recognition for a series of artworks entitled DADDY GOD. These images of a spiritual figure are based on the physical form of Avatar Meher Baba. Jameson is widely recognized among the followers of Meher Baba to be one of the most popular, prolific and well-known painters of the physical form of Avatar Meher Baba. He has been selected as the illustrator-of-choice by several of Meher Baba's closest Mandali (spiritual disciples); including Meher Baba's sister Mani Irani and Katie Irani. He first illustrated God Brother (1994), a children's book by Mani Irani, sister of the Indian spiritual master Avatar Meher Baba. Later, Mani Irani again chose him to illustrate her last published book, Dreaming of the Beloved (1998), by Mani Irani. He won the Printing Industry of the Carolinas (PICA)'s First Place for a 4-color book illustration. Fellow disciple of Meher Baba, Katie Irani then selected Jameson to illustrate her book of anecdotes & recipes calledCooking with Katie (2003), by Katie Irani.
Wang was born in Chun'an County, Zhejiang province, China. Wang obtained his B.S. in 1983 and his Ph.D. in 1990 from the Department of Mathematics of Zhejiang University (ZJU) in Hangzhou.

There is little scientific evidence to guide the care of the ear after tubes have been inserted. A single, randomized trial found statistical benefit to using ear protective devices when swimming although the size of the benefit was quite small. In the absence of strong evidence, general opinion has been against the use of ear protection devices. However, protection such as cotton covered with petroleum jelly, ear plugs, or ear putty is recommended for swimming in dirty water (lakes, rivers, oceans, or non-chlorinated pools) to prevent ear infections. For bathing, shampooing, or surface-water swimming in chlorinated pools, no ear protection is recommended.

As of the 2000 census, Lower Nazareth Township, Pennsylvania, had a population of 5,463, with 1,973 households and 1,596 families residing in the township. The population density was approximately 743.2 people per square mile, with 2,031 housing units at an average density of 276.3 per square mile. The racial makeup was 97.6% White, and 1.2% of the population identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race. The township's suburban charm and proximity to retail centers have contributed to its appeal as a growing community within Northampton County.

The FDA approved trastuzumab deruxtecan based on the results of one clinical trial enrolling 184 female participants with HER2-positive, unresectable and/or metastatic breast cancer who had received two or more prior anti-HER2 therapies in the metastatic setting. These participants were heavily pretreated in the metastatic setting, receiving between two and 17 therapies prior to receiving trastuzumab deruxtecan. Participants in the clinical trial received trastuzumab deruxtecan every three weeks and tumor imaging was obtained every six weeks. The overall response rate was 60.3%, which reflects the percentage of participants who had a certain amount of tumor shrinkage with a median duration of response of 14.8 months.

The jurisdiction of the court seems to have varied. At one time it encompassed the counties of Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Leicestershire and Warwickshire but by the fifth year of the reign of Charles I (c. 1630) it was limited to Staffordshire and Derbyshire. All minstrels within the court's jurisdiction were compelled to attend or else pay a fine which, by 1630, was four shillings and six pence. In one story the legendary outlaw Robin Hood is said to have attended the court.

The promotion of *Encore un soir* began with Celine Dion's Summer Tour 2016, announced on her official website in January 2016. The tour, which ran from 20 June to 31 August 2016, included 28 shows across Canada and France. Several songs from the *Encore un soir* album were performed live for the first time during this tour, including the title track "Encore un soir" and "Les yeux au ciel." On 29 June 2016, Dion performed these songs at the AccorHotels Arena in Paris, marking their live debut. Later, on 24 August 2016, she performed "Encore un soir" and "Les yeux au ciel" for the first time on television during M6's *Music Show – 100% tubes 2016*. Additionally, she appeared on *Le Grand Show* on France 2, which aired in late October 2016, to perform these tracks again. The album's promotion continued into 2017 with the *Celine Dion Live 2017* tour, her first European tour in over eight years, which supported the album and included performances of "Encore un soir." This tour spanned 25 shows across 15 cities in Europe, further solidifying the album's success.

Upon learning of the Entebbe raid, Idi Amin was furious and reportedly boasted that he could have taught the Israelis a lesson had he known they would strike. In retaliation, Amin accused 14 soldiers of collaborating with the Israelis. Colonel Maliyamungu gathered the soldiers in a room at Makindye Barracks and shot 12 of them with his pistol. Uganda Army Chief of Staff Mustafa Adrisi reportedly sought to incarcerate or execute Godwin Sule, the Entebbe Air Base commander, for his absence during the raid. Sule had left the base on 4 July to meet a female companion at Lake Victoria Hotel. However, his close relationship with Amin ensured his safety despite Adrisi's demands.

Larson grew up in Asheville, North Carolina, and attended Carolina Day School. Upon graduation from high school, she matriculated at Rochester Institute of Technology and then transferred to the School of the Art Institute of Chicago, where she graduated with a BFA in 2004. She then moved to Toronto with her husband, Canadian cartoonist Bryan Lee O'Malley. In 2005, they moved to Halifax, Nova Scotia.

Cyrus Harris, born August 22, 1817, in Pontotoc, Mississippi, moved to Mill Creek in 1855. The first governor of the Chickasaw Nation, he was re-elected four times. Harris lived near Mill Creek until his death in 1888 and was buried in Murray County, Oklahoma.
Xu-Jia Wang, born in Chun'an County, Zhejiang Province, obtained his B.S. degree in mathematics in 1983 and Ph.D. in 1990 from Zhejiang University.

Hierodoris tygris, a moth species endemic to New Zealand, is found in various regions, including Manurewa and Wellington. Its type locality is Manurewa, where it has been collected at the Auckland Botanic Gardens. Additionally, it has been recorded in Wellington's Wainuiomata Valley.

The increasing use of ICTs in the workplace has presented organizations with new ethical challenges. It has been argued that ICT use in organizational settings can contribute to counterproductive behaviour and deviancy as the line between personal and professional lives becomes blurred. Usually this behaviour consists of non-sanctioned use of ICTs during work hours, such as updating personal blogs, playing games, doing personal banking online, and using email for non-work related activities.

The Very Short-Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS) is a man-portable air defense system developed by the Research Centre Imarat (RCI) in collaboration with other DRDO laboratories and Indian industry partners. Designed to neutralize low-altitude aerial threats, it uses a dual-thrust solid rocket motor and features an Imaging Infra-Red (IIR) seeker. Two successful launches were conducted on February 28 and 29, 2024, at the Integrated Test Range, Chandipur, Odisha.

David Zurawik of The Baltimore Sun said the "most impressive accomplishment" of the film is the complete impartiality in the way Shepard portrays the Boy Scouts as an institution. Zurawik went on to note that even though Shepard reveals the inconsistency and sanctimoniousness between what the scouts claim are their stated values and its anti-gay policy, the film "still celebrates the idea of scouting and the incredible role it plays in [boys and young mens lives] in the United States". Film Threat praised Shepard for his portrayal of "Steven Cozza as just a normal kid who had the good fortune to be raised by a couple of great parents...it's unfathomable that a young man could take on such a sensitive cause, in a truly insensitive age bracket...and be accepted for it". Donald Liebenson of the Chicago Tribune remarked that the film is not an all out tirade against the Boy Scouts, but instead, it explores "the irony that the values Cozza took from scouting are what inspired him to take his stand".

She is a graduate of Parsippany Hills High School in Parsippany–Troy Hills, New Jersey, in the United States. She earned her Bachelor of Science in Education degree from Ateneo de Zamboanga University and her Masters in family counseling from Ateneo de Manila University. She was a guidance counselor and taught English and Religion at the Ateneo de Zamboanga University.
