Emilio Materassi made his racing debut in 1919 at the Coppa della Consuma, driving an Isotta Fraschini, likely provided by Maserati. Despite a flat tyre, he finished third in his class. In June 1922, he placed 8th overall at Mugello.

Interstate 229 (I-229) begins at a cloverleaf interchange with Interstate 29 (I-29) and US 71 in Buchanan County, Missouri. From this interchange, I-229 heads north, meeting Route A at a partial interchange with limited access—northbound I-229 traffic cannot exit to Route A. North of this interchange lies the Missouri River, while south is the St. Joseph Stockyards. The next half-diamond interchange provides access to Route 752, though northbound I-229 traffic cannot directly access Route 752; full access is available via Route 371, locally known as Stockyards Expressway.

White Wolf originally formed in 1975 under the name Slamm, then changed to Warrior before settling on White Wolf. The group played locally for several years before landing a recording contract with RCA Records; their debut album, Standing Alone, was issued on the label in 1984. The band toured in Canada, the United States, Europe and Japan in support of the album.

Gene DeWeese was born on January 31, 1934, in Rochester, Indiana. He began writing in grade school, attempting a sequel to a Mickey Mouse story inspired by Ray Cummings, and later contributed articles and science fiction columns to his local newspaper during high school. His early stories were also published in amateur science fiction magazines, marking his involvement in science fiction fandom. DeWeese earned an associate degree in electronics from Valparaiso Technical Institute in 1953. He worked as an electronics technician for General Motors' Delco Electronics Division in Kokomo, Indiana, from 1954 to 1959, before transitioning to a role as a technical writer in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, where he lived and worked from 1959 to 1974.

Kington Rural is intersected by five bus routes, all focused on the nearby market town of Kington. These routes are operated by Sargeants Brothers. Two of the routes run southeast to northwest along the A44, connecting Hereford to Llandrindod Wells and Hereford to Knighton, respectively. A circular route starts in Kington and travels through Lyonshall, Pembridge, Eardisland, and Shobdon before returning to Kington. Another route begins in Knighton and passes through Presteigne and Kington before continuing to Hereford. Within Kington town and Kington Rural, a fifth route provides local service, linking key stops such as High Street, Bridge Street, and Cutterbach Lane. The closest railway connections to Kington Rural are in England, with Leominster station located 10 miles (20 minutes) away and Hereford station 15 miles (25 minutes) away. Knighton railway station, just across the Welsh border, is also accessible and lies northeast of Kington Rural.

The Edgewater Beach Co-op Apartments, built in 1928 at the north end of the property, and shown in the photo at right, is the only part of the hotel complex to survive and is part of the Bryn Mawr Historic District. As he had before with many his other projects, such as the South Shore Country Club, the Blackstone Hotel, the Drake Hotel and Drake Tower, architect Benjamin Marshall designed the apartment building with accoutrements suited for the well-to-do. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1994. The apartments stand at the north end of Lake Shore Drive, quite visible to the passing traffic, and unusual in Chicago for the "sunset pink" exterior. When both buildings stood, the color coordinated with the "sunrise yellow" of the hotel.

The music video, directed by Niels Alpert, is set at a high school. Emerson Hart is shown both as a janitor in the hallway and playing with his band in the gymnasium. Scenes with cheerleaders wearing uniforms with the word LOVE in the gym are interspersed throughout the video. The principal's office and biology class are also shown, while students walk out of the building and make out on the quad at the end of the video.

The level of scientific evidence guiding ear care after tube insertion is limited, though one study demonstrated a statistical benefit to using ear protective devices when swimming, particularly in reducing the risk of infections like swimmer's ear. While the benefit was modest, ear protection is generally recommended in the absence of strong evidence, especially for children with tubes or frequent infections. Custom-fit ear plugs or swim ear plugs are advised for swimming in untreated or poorly maintained water, such as natural bodies of water or inadequately treated pools. However, ear protection is not typically necessary for bathing, shampooing, or surface-water swimming in chlorinated pools, as these activities pose minimal risk.

The 1998 Football League Second Division play-off final took place on Sunday, 24 May 1998, with a 3:00 pm kick-off at Wembley Stadium in London. The match, attended by nearly 70,000 spectators, saw Grimsby Town secure promotion to Division 1 with a 1-0 victory over Northampton Town. Kevin Donovan scored the only goal of the game in the first half, capitalizing on a pass from Paul Groves to give the Mariners the lead. Northampton's goalkeeper, Andy Woodman, was unable to stop Donovan's effort, which was later described by some as "the only highlight of a dreadfully dull first half." Shortly after the goal, Grimsby had a chance to double their lead when Jack Lester found himself one-on-one with Woodman, but his shot went wide. Despite Northampton's efforts to respond, they struggled to create significant opportunities, with Chris Freestone providing their only notable chance in the first half.

Kader is a prominent activist working in the fight for greater recognition of children’s rights. She has promoted youth empowerment through events such as the Montessori Model United Nations Conferences in April 2011 and 2012, youth interfaith dialogue events in addition to hosting several panel discussions on trafficking in children. She serves as an Ambassador and an Auxiliary Board Member of the CIS, specifically supporting the Virtual Global Task Force, which was the first ever international operational law enforcement task force to protect children worldwide over the Internet.

The music video for "Better Believe" was released to Belly's YouTube channel on July 22, 2021, and was directed by Christian Breslauer. The glossy, action-packed video is part one of a two-part series. It features self-drifting luxury cars, including a McLaren P1 driven by The Weeknd, who is later seen walking through explosions. Belly is shown on fire, while Young Thug appears with a super slimy tank truck, adding to the video's high-energy, cinematic visuals.

Tunnell was born in Bryn Mawr, Pennsylvania, though sources conflict as to his year of birth. His tombstone as well as the Social Security Death Index and Tunnell's Department of Veterans Affairs BIRLS Death File reflect a birth date of March 29, 1924. His *Cantica Sacra* (1618) includes 6-part motets that reflect a more conventionally expressive, Italian madrigal-like style.

Lachlan Chisholm Wilson was buried on 9 April 1947 at Toowong Cemetery, Brisbane. His funeral was attended by family, friends, and senior military personnel, including Major General John Lavarack and Brigadier William Glasgow, reflecting his esteemed military career and legacy.

As a consequence of Katanga attaining the Pedicle, it gained a toehold in the Bangweulu Wetlands and potential mineral resources, although as it turned out, the division of the main copper orebody between the Congo and Northern Rhodesia was determined by the Congo–Zambezi watershed and would not have been affected by the existence or otherwise of the Pedicle. It was the BSAC's failure to get Msiri to sign up Garanganza as a British protectorate which lost the Congolese Copperbelt to Northern Rhodesia, and some in the BSAC complained that the British missionaries Frederick Stanley Arnot and Charles Swan could have done more to help, although their Plymouth Brethren mission had a policy of not being involved in politics. Once Msiri was killed by the CFS it was too late to try again, and consequently the leader of CFS expedition responsible, Canadian Captain William Grant Stairs was viewed by some in Northern Rhodesia as a traitor to the British Empire.

George Hebard Maxwell, a prominent advocate for irrigation and land reclamation, argued for the comprehensive damming of the Colorado River in his book *Patriotism of Peace* (1907), citing the need to prevent flooding and support agricultural development. He controversially claimed that Baja California was being colonized by Asians and referred to Southern and Eastern European immigrants as unsuitable for American society. Maxwell promoted Homecroft communities to assimilate these immigrants and supported policies restricting land ownership by Asians in California, reflecting his nativist and exclusionary views.

In April 2004, following the Turkish Cypriot community's support for the UN-led unification plan in a referendum, the EU pledged to end their isolation. The European Commission proposed direct trade regulations to grant Turkish Cypriot goods access to EU markets. However, the proposal faced opposition from Greek Cypriot leaders and MEPs, who argued it would weaken their negotiating position in the Cyprus dispute. Legal challenges and political disagreements stalled implementation, with discussions resurfacing in the European Parliament in June 2010.

The first written record of the plant in South Africa is by Jan van Riebeeck, who ordered officers of the Voorman to purchase "daccha" in Natal for trade with the Khoikhoi. The Dutch East India Company attempted to establish a monopoly on its sale, and to that end prohibited cultivation of the plant by Cape settlers from 1680. However, the ready availability of cannabis in the wild and through trade with indigenous peoples meant that there was little profit to be made. Consequently, the prohibition was lifted in 1700.

Trompies released their studio album *Delicious* in 2014, which was nominated at the 21st South African Music Awards in 2015. In 2021, they received four nominations at the Mzansi Kwaito and House Music Awards in categories including Best Kwaito/House/Gqom Group and Best Kwaito Single.

Cathal Pendred began his professional mixed martial arts career in 2009. In 2010, he won the Irish national welterweight title and became the top-ranked welterweight in Ireland. In March 2013, he defeated Gael Grimaud to claim the Cage Warriors Welterweight Championship.

The Joy Luck Club received widespread critical acclaim upon its release. On Rotten Tomatoes, the film holds an impressive 90% "Fresh" rating based on 51 reviews, with 46 of them classified as "fresh." The site's consensus praises the film for its "powerful performances and rich storytelling," highlighting its emotional depth and cultural significance. Audiences also responded positively, giving the film an "A" grade on CinemaScore. Renowned critic Gene Siskel commended the film's script and performances, emphasizing its ability to explore universal themes of family and identity while offering a rare and authentic portrayal of Chinese-American experiences. Siskel ranked The Joy Luck Club as the third-best film of 1993, while his co-host Roger Ebert placed it fourth on his list. Both critics celebrated the film as one of the year's finest, with Siskel particularly noting its success in making the personal stories of its characters resonate on a universal level. The film's critical and audience reception solidified its status as a landmark in Asian-American cinema.

In introducing federal discrimination protections for LGBTI people, the Gillard Government promised that religious bodies would be exempt, unless they were aged care providers receiving Commonwealth funding. The main exemptions are in sections 37 and 38 of the Sex Discrimination Act 1984, which include the ability for religious educational institutions to discriminate against LGBT students and teachers "in good faith in order to avoid injury to the religious susceptibilities of adherents of that religion". In 2017, Perth teacher Craig Campbell was dismissed from a Baptist school after he revealed his sexuality on social media. The Greens promised to repeal religious exemptions to LGBT anti-discrimination protections before the 2016 election, while in January 2018 the Labor Party announced it had no plans to act upon the issue. In the wake of the same-sex marriage debate, the Turnbull Government commissioned a review of religious freedoms chaired by Phillip Ruddock, after conservative Coalition politicians called for increased religious freedoms to discriminate against LGBT people. The need to protect religious freedom was emphasised by Scott Morrison after he replaced Malcolm Turnbull as Prime Minister.

Jennifer Paige's debut single, "Crush," co-written by Andy Goldmark, Mark Mueller, Berny Cosgrove, and Kevin Clark, was first played by KIIS-FM in Los Angeles during the summer of 1998. The song quickly became the station's most requested track, despite minimal promotion. "Crush" peaked at #3 on the Billboard Hot 100 in the U.S., remaining in the Top 10 for over two months. Internationally, it reached #1 in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand, solidifying its status as a global pop hit.

In 1998, Daniel Subotnik published one of the earliest critiques of critical race theory (CRT) in his article, *"What's Wrong with Critical Race Theory: Reopening the Case for Middle Class Values,"* in the *Cornell Journal of Law and Public Policy*. Subotnik highlighted exaggerated claims within the CRT community while acknowledging its valid critiques of systemic racism. He warned that modern race relations risk being undermined by divisive rhetoric and argued that white academics must actively engage in CRT discourse. Subotnik also urged CRT scholars to confront the complexities of a racially diverse society, emphasizing the need for constructive dialogue to address the evolving racial landscape in America.

In 1960, Skomvær was given a new lease of life when Norwegian folk singer and television and radio presenter Erik Bye released his first studio album, Vi går ombord. The sixth track on the album, "Skomværsvalsen" (Template:Lang-en), which was co-written with Bjarne Amdahl, became one of Bye's biggest hits and received heavy rotation on the NRK radio show Ønskekonserten. The track gained additional popularity from being featured on the annual NRK Christmas broadcast "Vi går ombord," where the song was performed by the Norwegian Seamen's Choir. The song was recorded as a tribute to Skomvær and her crew, with its well-known opening line "And the ship was named Skomvær in Porsgrunn city."

The Port Office Hotel, located on the corner of Edward and Margaret Streets in Brisbane, is a two-storeyed rendered brick building with a corrugated iron roof. The brickwork is scribed to imitate stonework, with bays separated by rendered quoins, and a cantilevered awning dominates the street facades.

Ilse Hülper was born in Germany and received her vocal training at a conservatory, later attending an acting school to refine her performance skills. She gained her first stage experience in 1938 at a local theater. From 1940 to 1942, she was engaged as a soloist at the Staatstheater Cottbus before moving on to another theater for further engagements. From 1945 onwards, she performed at various theaters in Berlin, showcasing a repertoire that included opera, operetta, and concert performances. Her career spanned diverse roles and productions, reflecting her versatility as a performer. Ilse Hülper concluded her stage career in 1960, leaving behind a legacy of artistic contributions to German theater and music.

ABC News Now was a 24-hour cable news network that launched on July 26, 2004, as a digital subchannel by ABC News, being the company's second attempt in the 24-hour cable news world after Satellite News Channel. It was offered via digital television, broadband and streaming video at ABCNews.com and on mobile phones. It delivered breaking news, headline news each half hour, and wide range of entertainment and lifestyle programming. The channel was available in the United States and Europe. Its Talk Back feature allowed viewers to voice their own input through the submission of videos and personal thoughts on controversial issues and current topics. It was shut down as a digital subchannel when after its experimental phase had ended with the President inauguration in 2005. ABC News Now was replaced on cable providers with Fusion on October 28, 2013.

Throughout his career with Dream Theater, LaBrie has lent his voice to many other artists' records as well as tribute albums. In 1991, not long after joining Dream Theater (and before ever appearing on a Dream Theater recording), he sang background vocals on the song "Life in Still Water" on Fates Warning's Parallels album. He has appeared on many Trent Gardner releases (including Leonardo: The Absolute Man and Explorer's Club), as well as appearing on albums by such artists as Shadow Gallery, Tim Donahue and Frameshift.

The Debut originated as a 10-minute short film created by Gene Cajayon in 1992 as his thesis project at Loyola Marymount University. Co-written with John Manal Castro, the feature-length film incorporated themes from Castro's short film *The Flip Side*. Production spanned over a decade, involving extensive fundraising and 21 script drafts. After years of development, *The Debut* premiered in 2000 and was released in theaters in 2001, showcasing Filipino-American culture and identity.

The Royal Botanic Garden, Sydney, spans 29 hectares and is surrounded by 51 hectares of open parklands known as The Domain. Situated on the edge of Sydney Harbour, the garden forms a natural amphitheatre around Farm Cove, sloping down towards the water. It is divided into four major precincts: the Lower Gardens, the Middle Gardens, the Palace Gardens, and the Bennelong precinct. At the heart of these precincts lies the Palm Grove Centre, which serves as a hub for visitors. The centre offers a range of facilities, including a restaurant, café, visitor centre, and bookshop, making it a focal point for both leisure and education.

Kanapacki died on 9 September 2005. After his death, several Belarusian newspapers, including Nasha Niva, paid tribute to him. In 2014, a plaque at his family home in Smilavičy home was placed commemorating him.

General Motors / Eastern Aircraft produced 5,280 FM variants of the Wildcat. Grumman's Wildcat production ceased in early 1943 to make way for the newer F6F Hellcat, but General Motors continued producing Wildcats for both U.S. Navy and Fleet Air Arm use. Late in the war, the Wildcat was obsolescent as a front line fighter compared to the faster (380 mph/610 km/h) F6F Hellcat or much faster (446 mph/718 km/h) F4U Corsair. However, they were adequate for small escort carriers against submarine and shore threats. These relatively modest ships only carried two types of aircraft, the Wildcats and GM-built TBM Avengers. The Wildcat's lower landing speed and ability to take off without a catapult made it more suitable for shorter flight decks. At first, GM produced the FM-1, identical to the F4F-4, but reduced the number of guns to four, and added wing racks for two 250 lb (110 kg) bombs or six rockets. Production later switched to the improved FM-2 (based on Grumman's XF4F-8 prototype) optimized for small-carrier operations, with a more powerful engine (the 1,350 hp (1,010 kW) Wright R-1820-56), and a taller tail to cope with the torque.
